# Delete Account
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/account/delete

Permanently delete your Oviond account and all associated data.

Deleting an account is **permanent and irreversible**. Every client, project, widget, integration, automation, theme, template, and team member in the account is deleted.

<Warning>
  Account deletion is not a soft-delete. There is no Archive recovery for an entire account. Export any reports you need to keep, and confirm you've ended any active subscription before proceeding.
</Warning>

## Before you delete

* **Export critical reports** — PDF every report you want to retain ([PDF Export](/automations/pdf)).
* **Cancel your subscription** — see [Subscription](/billing/subscription). Deleting an account doesn't automatically cancel billing.
* **Revoke API keys** — integrations using your account's API keys will stop working immediately.
* **Notify your team** — every user in the account loses access the moment you confirm.

## How to delete

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Account">
    From the sidebar, click your avatar → **Account Settings**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Scroll to Delete Account">
    At the bottom of the page.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Delete Account">
    You'll be prompted to type your account name to confirm.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Confirm">
    Oviond deletes the account and its data immediately.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  Only the account **owner** can delete an account. Admins and other roles don't see the Delete Account button.
</Note>


# Account
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/account/overview

Your Oviond account — the agency workspace that holds every client, project, and team member.

Your account settings let you control everything personal to your login — your display name, language, avatar, and email address — as well as account-level details that apply across your entire Oviond workspace. You can also monitor how much of your plan you've used and, if needed, delete your account entirely.

To reach your account settings, click your avatar or initials in the bottom-left corner of the sidebar, then select **Account Settings**.

## Update your profile

Your profile controls how you appear inside Oviond and, where applicable, in client-facing communications sent from your account.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open your profile settings">
    Navigate to **Account Settings** and select the **Profile** tab.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Update your name">
    Edit the **First name** and **Last name** fields. Your full name appears in the Oviond interface and in team member listings.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose your language">
    Use the **Language** dropdown to select your preferred display language. This changes the language of the Oviond interface for your login only — it does not affect your teammates' language preferences or what your clients see.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Upload an avatar">
    Click your current avatar image (or the initials placeholder) and select an image file from your computer. Oviond accepts JPG and PNG files. Your avatar is visible to other team members inside the platform.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save your changes">
    Click **Save** to apply your updates.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Change your email address

Your email address is used to log in to Oviond and to receive platform notifications such as scheduled report confirmations and billing receipts.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open your profile settings">
    Go to **Account Settings** and select the **Profile** tab.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enter your new email address">
    Locate the **Email address** field and type your new email.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save your changes">
    Click **Save**. Oviond may send a verification email to your new address to confirm the change.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  After changing your email, use the new address the next time you log in.
</Note>

## Account-level settings

Account-level settings apply to your entire Oviond workspace — not just your personal profile. Only the account owner or users with admin permissions can edit these settings.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open account details">
    Go to **Account Settings** and select the **Account** tab.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Update your company name">
    Edit the **Company name** field. This name can appear in reports and white-label configurations depending on how your branding is set up.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Review other account details">
    Update any other account-level fields shown, such as your website or contact information.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save your changes">
    Click **Save** to apply the updates across your workspace.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## View usage vs. plan limits

Oviond enforces limits on the number of clients, reports (projects), and data integrations you can have active at one time, based on your current plan. You can check your usage at any time to see how much of each limit you've consumed.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the usage overview">
    Go to **Account Settings** and select the **Usage** tab (sometimes listed under **Plan & Usage**).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Review your usage">
    The usage panel displays the following metrics alongside your plan's limits:

    | Resource               | Description                                                                |
    | ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
    | **Clients**            | The number of client workspaces you've created vs. your plan's maximum.    |
    | **Reports / Projects** | The total number of reports and dashboards across all clients.             |
    | **Integrations**       | The number of active data source connections (e.g., Google Ads, Facebook). |
    | **Team members**       | The number of invited team members vs. your seat limit.                    |
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  If you're approaching a limit, you can upgrade your plan from **Billing** in the sidebar. See [Plans and billing](/billing/plans) for details on what each plan includes.
</Note>

## Delete your account

Deleting your account permanently removes your Oviond workspace, including all clients, reports, integrations, and team member access. This action cannot be undone.

<Warning>
  Account deletion is irreversible. All your data — clients, reports, dashboards, integrations, and team members — will be permanently deleted. Make sure to export any reports or data you need before proceeding. If you're on a paid plan, deleting your account does not automatically issue a refund; contact [support@oviond.com](mailto:support@oviond.com) before deleting if you have billing questions.
</Warning>

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open account settings">
    Go to **Account Settings** and select the **Account** tab.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Find the delete option">
    Scroll to the bottom of the page and click **Delete account**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Confirm deletion">
    Oviond will ask you to confirm by typing your account name or email address. Enter the required text and click **Confirm delete**.
  </Step>
</Steps>

Your account and all associated data will be removed immediately after confirmation.


# Usage
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/account/usage

See what your agency has used against your plan's limits.

The **Usage** panel shows how much of your plan's quota you've consumed this billing period. Access it from **Account → Usage**.

## What's tracked

| Metric              | Limit                                                    |
| ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Clients**         | Number of active clients (archived clients don't count). |
| **Projects**        | Total reports and dashboards across all clients.         |
| **Team members**    | Active users in your account.                            |
| **API requests**    | Calls to the Oviond REST API this month.                 |
| **Emails sent**     | Automation deliveries this month.                        |
| **PDF generations** | Exported PDFs this month.                                |

Each metric shows **used / limit** with a progress bar. If you're close to a limit, Oviond surfaces a warning in the app header.

## Over-limit behavior

* **Hard limits** (clients, projects, team members) block new creation once exceeded — existing records keep working.
* **Soft limits** (API requests, emails, PDFs) temporarily throttle or queue new activity — they don't remove anything.

See [Plans](/billing/plans) for limit details per plan tier, or [Subscription](/billing/subscription) to upgrade.


# Activity Log
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/activity-log/overview

Audit every meaningful action taken in your account.

The **Activity Log** records who did what and when, across projects, clients, integrations, users, and billing events. Use it to:

* Trace when a report was created, updated, or deleted
* See which team member invited a user or changed a role
* Correlate a data anomaly with a recent integration reconnect

Entries retain the actor (`user_id`), target (`resource_type`, `resource_id`), and timestamp. Filter by user or resource type from the Activity Log view.


# Add Project
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/agency/add

Create an account-level agency report or dashboard.

An **agency report** is a project that lives at the account level — no client scope. Use them for internal dashboards, pitch decks, or cross-client rollups.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Agency screen">
    Click **Agency** in the left sidebar. This lists every account-level project.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click + New Agency Report">
    Opens the create dialog — no client picker since agency reports aren't scoped to a client.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Name your project">
    The name appears on the shared link, in PDF exports, and in automation email subjects.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick the type">
    **Report** (paged) or **Dashboard** (single-scroll).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Start blank or from a template">
    Templates work the same way they do for client projects — see [Apply Template](/templates/apply).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Create">
    Oviond opens the editor.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Data sources

Agency reports pull from the agency's **shared integrations** — data sources connected at the account level rather than to a specific client. Connect them under [Data Sources](/data-sources/list) and they become available to every agency report.


# Archive Project
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/agency/archive

Soft-delete an agency report with full restore within the retention window.

Archiving an agency report removes it from your active Agency list without permanently destroying its pages, widgets, or date-range settings. Restore it from the [Archive](/archive/view) any time before the retention window expires.

## Archive a report

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the menu">
    From the Agency screen, click the three-dot menu on the report card, or open the report and use its header menu.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Archive">
    The report disappears from the active Agency list and moves to the [Archive](/archive/view).
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Restore

From the Archive, find the report, click **Restore**, and it reappears in the Agency list with all pages and widgets intact.

## Retention

Archived agency reports older than the retention window (90 days by default) are **permanently purged** by a background job. For long-term storage of reference reports, [duplicate](/agency/duplicate) into a dedicated folder or rename the copy clearly.


# Bulk Delete
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/agency/bulk-delete

Remove multiple agency reports at once.

Bulk delete speeds up cleanup when you're pruning a batch of old agency reports — e.g. retired pitch decks or stale internal dashboards.

<Warning>
  Deleted agency reports move to the [Archive](/archive/view) along with all their pages and widgets. They can be restored within the retention window; after that, they're purged permanently. Export anything you need first.
</Warning>

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Agency screen">
    From the left sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enter selection mode">
    Hover a report card and click the checkbox that appears, or toggle **Select** mode from the toolbar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick the reports">
    Check every report you want to delete.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Delete">
    Click **Delete selected** in the action bar and confirm. Reports move to the Archive.
  </Step>
</Steps>


# Duplicate Project
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/agency/duplicate

Copy an agency report — pages, widgets, and settings — under a new name.

Duplicating creates an exact clone of an agency report, including every page, widget, and setting. Useful for:

* Spinning up a new variant (e.g. quarterly vs. monthly version)
* Testing layout changes on a copy before applying to the live report
* Creating a forked version for a different internal team

<Steps>
  <Step title="Find the report">
    On the Agency screen, locate the agency report you want to copy.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the menu">
    Click the three-dot menu next to the report.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Duplicate">
    Oviond creates a copy named `Copy of [original]`.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Rename">
    Open the copy's settings to give it a new name. See [Update Project](/agency/update).
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Tip>
  If you want a reusable starting point rather than a one-off copy, [create a Template](/templates/add) from the agency report instead — templates keep a library you can re-apply to any future project.
</Tip>


# List Projects
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/agency/list

Browse every account-level agency report.

The **Agency** screen lists every agency report in your account. These aren't scoped to a client, so they don't appear under any client's workspace.

## Filter and search

* **Search** — match by project name.
* **Type filter** — show only **Reports** or only **Dashboards**.
* **Sort** — switch between recent activity, name, and date created.

## What's on a card

| Element         | Description                        |
| --------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| **Name**        | Click to open the editor.          |
| **Type**        | Report or Dashboard badge.         |
| **Last edited** | Timestamp of the most recent save. |

## Quick actions

From any agency-report card's three-dot menu:

* **Open** — enter the editor.
* **Duplicate** — see [Duplicate Project](/agency/duplicate).
* **Share** — see [Share Project](/agency/share).
* **Archive** — see [Archive Project](/agency/archive).


# Pages
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/agency/pages

Organize an agency report into tabs — one page per section.

A **page** is a tab inside an agency report. Each page has its own grid layout and holds any number of widgets. Viewers switch between pages to explore different sections — e.g. one page per internal KPI group.

Every agency report starts with at least one page. Add more at any time.

## Add a page

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the agency report">
    Click the report on the Agency screen.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click + Add Page">
    The button sits in the page tab bar at the top of the editor.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Rename it">
    Double-click the tab to rename. Use clear names like *MRR & ARR* or *Team Performance*.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Reorder and hide pages

* **Reorder** — drag a page tab left or right to change its position.
* **Hide** — from the page's three-dot menu, toggle **Hidden** to drop it from the viewer-facing view without deleting it.

## Delete a page

Open the page's three-dot menu and choose **Delete**. Every widget on the page is removed with the page.

<Tip>
  Plan pages before adding widgets. Grouping related KPIs per page keeps agency reports scannable for execs and internal stakeholders.
</Tip>


# Share Project
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/agency/share

Publish an agency report via a shareable public link.

Share an agency report with a teammate, stakeholder, or prospect via a public URL. The recipient opens the link in any browser — **no Oviond account required** — and sees live data.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the agency report">
    From the Agency screen.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Share">
    The Share button sits in the top-right of the editor. A full link and a short link are both generated.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enable the public link">
    Toggle **Public link** on.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Copy the link">
    Full URL or short URL — short URLs are easier to share in chat or email.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Optional: password-protect">
    Enable **Password** under the share settings and set a passphrase. Recipients are prompted for it before the report loads.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Custom domain

If you've set up a [Custom Domain](/custom-domains/list), shared links use your white-label domain instead of `app.oviond.com`. Logo, favicon, and brand colors render on the share page automatically.

## Live vs snapshot

Public links render live data every time they're opened. To send a static snapshot, use [PDF Export](/automations/pdf).

<Warning>
  Anyone with the link can view the report. Use password protection if the agency report contains sensitive internal data.
</Warning>


# Update Project
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/agency/update

Rename and configure settings on an agency report.

Agency-report settings cover everything outside the canvas — name, date range defaults, and PDF export options. There's no client reassignment since agency reports aren't scoped to a client.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the agency report">
    From the Agency screen, click the report you want to edit.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open Project Settings">
    Click the **Settings** icon in the editor or choose **Project Settings** from the three-dot menu.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Rename">
    Edit the **Project name** field. The new name appears on the shared link and PDF exports.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set the default date range">
    Pick a fixed range or a relative range (e.g. *Last 30 Days*) — every widget that uses the project-level date inherits this.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Configure PDF export">
    Under **PDF**, set paper size, orientation, and any header / footer content.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    Click **Save** to apply.
  </Step>
</Steps>


# Widgets
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/agency/widgets

Charts, KPI tiles, tables, and text blocks that populate every page of an agency report.

A **widget** is an individual visualization on a page. Each widget connects to a data source and displays one metric or breakdown. Agency widgets pull from the agency's **shared integrations** rather than a specific client's data sources.

## Widget types

| Type            | Use for                                                           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **KPI tile**    | A single headline metric, optional period-over-period comparison. |
| **Chart**       | Trends over time — line, bar, or area.                            |
| **Table**       | Multiple metrics or dimensions side by side.                      |
| **Pie / donut** | Breakdown of a metric by dimension (e.g. revenue by team).        |
| **Text block**  | Freeform text — headings, commentary, summaries.                  |
| **Image**       | Inline images from the Media library or a URL.                    |
| **Embed**       | Embed any iframe-compatible source.                               |
| **Goal**        | Progress bar toward a target value.                               |
| **Button**      | A CTA link (e.g. to a sales page).                                |

## Add a widget

<Steps>
  <Step title="Select the page">
    Click the page tab you want to add the widget to.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the widget panel">
    Click **+ Add Widget** in the toolbar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose the type">
    KPI, Chart, Table, etc.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick a data source">
    Choose an agency-level [Data Source](/data-sources/list). Only sources connected at the account level are available — client-scoped integrations don't appear.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick a metric">
    Select the metric (and optional dimension).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set the date range">
    Fixed or relative (e.g. *Last 30 Days*) — relative ranges auto-update whenever the report is viewed.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Add to the page">
    Click **Add**. The widget appears on the canvas.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Configure a widget

Click any widget to open its settings panel on the right:

* **Data source / metric / dimension** — swap what the widget displays.
* **Date range** — override the project-level default for this widget.
* **Chart type** — switch visualizations without losing your data config.
* **Appearance** — title, colors, per-type display options.

## Rearrange and resize

Widgets use a drag-and-drop grid:

* **Move** — drag the widget header to a new position.
* **Resize** — drag the corner handle. Widgets snap to the grid.

## Bulk delete widgets

Hold **Shift** and click each widget to multi-select, then click **Delete selected** in the toolbar.

## Saving

Oviond uses an atomic save — **every page and every widget in the agency report** is saved in one operation. Click **Save** in the top-right of the editor before leaving.

<Warning>
  Navigating away without saving loses every unsaved widget change. Always save first.
</Warning>


# Add API Key
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api-keys/add

Generate a new API key to authenticate REST API calls.

API keys let external scripts and integrations call the Oviond REST API on behalf of your account. A key inherits the permissions of the user who created it — invite a dedicated read-only user for key-issuing if you want to scope-limit.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open API Keys">
    From **Account → API Keys**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click + New Key">
    Opens the create dialog.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Name the key">
    Use a descriptive name — e.g. *Zapier — Marketing Workflows* — so you know what depends on it.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Create">
    Oviond shows the key **once** — copy it immediately. We hash the key after this screen; there's no way to retrieve it later.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Paste into your tool">
    Use the key as a Bearer token: `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Warning>
  The full key is only visible at creation. If you lose it, you'll need to [revoke](/api-keys/revoke) the key and generate a new one.
</Warning>


# Delete API Key
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api-keys/delete

Permanently remove an API key from the list.

Deleting a key removes it entirely from your API Keys list — the audit entry is gone. If you just want to stop the key from working while keeping the audit trail, [Revoke](/api-keys/revoke) instead.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open API Keys">
    From **Account → API Keys**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the key's menu">
    Click the three-dot menu.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Delete">
    Confirm when prompted.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Warning>
  Deletion is irreversible and the key's metadata (name, created-at, created-by) is lost. Revoke first and delete later if you want to preserve the audit record.
</Warning>


# List API Keys
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api-keys/list

Every API key issued for your Oviond account.

Oviond API keys let you authenticate requests to the Oviond REST API from external tools, scripts, or integrations. Each key is tied to your account and operates within your permission level.

## Create an API key

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open API Keys settings">
    Click your account name in the top-right corner, select **Settings**, and go to the **API Keys** tab.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Create API Key">
    Click **Create API Key** and enter a descriptive name — for example, "Zapier workflow" or "Internal dashboard script".
  </Step>

  <Step title="Copy your key">
    Your new API key is displayed once. Copy it and store it somewhere secure.

    <Warning>
      You cannot view the full key again after closing this dialog. If you lose it, revoke the key and create a new one.
    </Warning>
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Use your API key

Include the key in the `Authorization` header on every API request:

```bash theme={null}
curl https://api.oviond.com/v1/users/me \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY"
```

See [Authentication](/authentication) for more details on how to authenticate API requests.

## Manage existing keys

From **Settings → API Keys** you can:

* **View** all active keys (name and creation date — the full key value is never shown again)
* **Revoke** a key — immediately invalidates it, any requests using it will receive `401 Unauthorized`
* **Delete** a key — permanently removes it from your account

<Note>
  Revoking a key is immediate. Update any integrations or scripts using that key before revoking to avoid downtime.
</Note>

## Security best practices

* Give each key a descriptive name so you can identify and revoke individual integrations
* Never commit API keys to version control
* Rotate keys periodically or immediately if you suspect a key has been exposed
* Use one key per integration so you can revoke a single key without affecting others


# Revoke API Key
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api-keys/revoke

Immediately disable an API key without deleting its audit record.

Revoking a key stops it from authenticating new requests — every future call returns `401 Unauthorized`. The key's metadata (name, created-at, created-by) stays in the list for audit.

Use revoke when:

* The key was accidentally shared or committed to a public repo
* A team member leaves the agency
* An integration is being decommissioned

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open API Keys">
    From **Account → API Keys**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the key's menu">
    Click the three-dot menu on the key row.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Revoke">
    Confirm when prompted.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Revoked vs deleted

| State       | Still in list? | Can still authenticate? | Can be restored?      |
| ----------- | -------------- | ----------------------- | --------------------- |
| **Revoked** | Yes            | No                      | No — create a new key |
| **Deleted** | No             | No                      | No                    |

Revoke first — deleting is irreversible and drops the audit record. See [Delete API Key](/api-keys/delete).


# Delete Account
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/account/delete-account

/api/openapi.json delete /v1/account
Irreversible. Cancels the active Stripe subscription, deletes all data across every table for this account, removes all team members from the users table, purges all S3 media files, and deletes all Supabase Auth users. Requires the account owner's current password as a confirmation gate.



# Get Account
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/account/get-account

/api/openapi.json get /v1/account
Get the accounts row for the current account



# Get Account Usage
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/account/get-account-usage

/api/openapi.json get /v1/account/usage
Get account usage statistics vs plan limits



# List Activity
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/activity/list-activity

/api/openapi.json get /v1/activity
List activity logs for the current account



# Delete Permanently
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/archive/delete-permanently

/api/openapi.json delete /v1/archive/permanent
Permanently delete archived items



# Get Archive Counts
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/archive/get-archive-counts

/api/openapi.json get /v1/archive/counts
Archived item counts per type



# List Archive
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/archive/list-archive

/api/openapi.json get /v1/archive
List archived items (paginated)



# Restore Items
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/archive/restore-items

/api/openapi.json put /v1/archive/restore
Restore soft-deleted items



# Create Asset
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/assets/create-asset

/api/openapi.json post /v1/assets
Create a new asset



# Delete Asset
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/assets/delete-asset

/api/openapi.json delete /v1/assets/:id
Delete an asset



# Duplicate Asset
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/assets/duplicate-asset

/api/openapi.json post /v1/assets/:id/duplicate
Duplicate an asset with a new name



# Get Asset
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/assets/get-asset

/api/openapi.json get /v1/assets/:id
Get a single asset by ID



# List Asset Tags
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/assets/list-asset-tags

/api/openapi.json get /v1/assets/tags
List all unique tags across assets for the current account



# List Asset Widgets
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/assets/list-asset-widgets

/api/openapi.json get /v1/assets/:id/widgets
List the widgets that belong to an asset



# List Assets
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/assets/list-assets

/api/openapi.json get /v1/assets
List assets for the current account



# Update Asset
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/assets/update-asset

/api/openapi.json put /v1/assets/:id
Update an asset

Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/automations/bulk-delete-automations

/api/openapi.json delete /v1/automations/bulk
Soft-delete multiple automations and remove them from the cron scheduler

/api/openapi.json post /v1/automations
Create a new automation for a project

/api/openapi.json delete /v1/automations/:id

/api/openapi.json get /v1/automations/:id/history

/api/openapi.json get /v1/automations
List automations across all projects for the current account



# Pause Automation
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/automations/pause-automation

/api/openapi.json put /v1/automations/:id/pause
Pause an automation (removes the cron job but retains the config)

/api/openapi.json post /v1/automations/:id/test
Trigger a test execution of an automation

/api/openapi.json put /v1/automations/:id/unpause
Resume a paused automation (re-registers the cron job)

/api/openapi.json delete /v1/email/smtp

/api/openapi.json post /v1/integrations/test

Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/integrations/update-client-integrations

/api/openapi.json put /v1/integrations/:client_id
Replace the integration rows for a client in client_integrations



# Overview
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/introduction

Explore the Oviond REST API. Authenticate with a Bearer token, manage reports, integrations, automations, and access public shared report data.

The Oviond REST API lets you programmatically manage your agency's reports, clients, integrations, and automations. All requests are made over HTTPS to `https://api.oviond.com`.

## Base URL

```
https://api.oviond.com
```

## Authentication

All API requests (except public endpoints) require a Bearer token in the `Authorization` header:

```bash theme={null}
Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY
```

Generate an API key from **Settings → API Keys** in the Oviond app. See [Authentication](/authentication) for full details.

## Request format

* Send JSON bodies with `Content-Type: application/json`
* All responses are JSON
* Timestamps use ISO 8601 format

## HTTP status codes

| Code  | Meaning                                   |
| ----- | ----------------------------------------- |
| `200` | Success                                   |
| `201` | Created                                   |
| `204` | No content (successful delete)            |
| `400` | Bad request — check your request body     |
| `401` | Unauthorized — missing or invalid API key |
| `403` | Forbidden — insufficient permissions      |
| `404` | Not found                                 |
| `422` | Validation error                          |
| `500` | Server error                              |

## Rate limiting

API requests are rate-limited per account. If you exceed the limit, you'll receive a `429 Too Many Requests` response. Wait before retrying.


# Bulk Delete Media
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/media/bulk-delete-media

/api/openapi.json delete /v1/media/bulk
Soft-delete multiple media items.



# Create Media Folder
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/media/create-media-folder

/api/openapi.json post /v1/media/folders
Create a new media folder.



# Delete Media
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/media/delete-media

/api/openapi.json delete /v1/media/:id
Soft-delete a media item. The underlying file is retained in S3 for potential restore.



# Delete Media Folder
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/media/delete-media-folder

/api/openapi.json delete /v1/media/folders/:id
Delete a media folder. Media items in the folder are not deleted.



# Group Media
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/media/group-media

/api/openapi.json put /v1/media/group
Move multiple media items to a folder



# List Media
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/media/list-media

/api/openapi.json get /v1/media
List media assets for the current account (paginated)



# List Media Folders
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/media/list-media-folders

/api/openapi.json get /v1/media/folders
List media folders for the current account.



# Rename Media Folder
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/media/rename-media-folder

/api/openapi.json put /v1/media/folders/:id
Rename a media folder.



# Update Media
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/media/update-media

/api/openapi.json put /v1/media/:id
Update media metadata (e.g. rename).



# Create Page
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/pages/create-page

/api/openapi.json post /v1/pages
Create a page on a project or template.



# Delete Page
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/pages/delete-page

/api/openapi.json delete /v1/pages/{page_id}
Delete a page.

/api/openapi.json get /v1/pages
List pages for a project or template.



# Update Page
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/pages/update-page

/api/openapi.json put /v1/pages/{page_id}
Update a page.

/api/openapi.json post /v1/pdf/generate
Enqueue a PDF generation job for a report.

/api/openapi.json get /v1/pdf/status/:job_id

/api/openapi.json delete /v1/projects/{id}

Get a single project by ID.

/api/openapi.json get /v1/projects/all
List all projects (id + name only) for dropdown selectors.

Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/projects/list-projects

/api/openapi.json get /v1/projects
List projects for the current account (optionally filtered by client).

/api/openapi.json put /v1/projects/{id}
Update a project.

/api/openapi.json put /v1/users/:id
Edit a team member (role, allowed projects, name).

/api/openapi.json post /v1/white-label/domains
Add a custom domain. Detects the DNS provider and registers the domain on Vercel.

# Update Widget
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/api/widgets/update-widget

/api/openapi.json put /v1/widgets/{id}
Update a single widget (partial update).



# Permanently Delete
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/archive/delete

Remove archived items from your account forever.

Permanently deleting an archived item is **irreversible** — the row is removed from the database along with all its children (a client's projects, a project's widgets, etc). Use it to:

* Free up plan quota immediately rather than waiting for the retention window
* Remove sensitive data after a client requests GDPR/CCPA erasure
* Clean up test data with certainty

<Warning>
  There is no undo. Once an item is permanently deleted, it cannot be recovered — not even by Oviond support.
</Warning>

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Archive">
    From **Account → Archive**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Filter and select">
    Narrow to the type you want to purge, then check each item.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Permanently Delete">
    Confirm by typing a verification word when prompted.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Automatic purge

Archived items older than the retention window (90 days by default) are **automatically purged** by a background job. You don't need to manually delete them — they're cleaned up periodically on Oviond's end.


# Restore
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/archive/restore

Bring a soft-deleted item back to your active workspace.

Restoring moves an item out of the Archive and back to its original location. Clients, projects, and media items can all be restored — and their dependent children (a client's projects, a project's widgets) come back intact.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Archive">
    From **Account → Archive**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Filter by type">
    Narrow to **Clients**, **Projects**, or **Media** if you know what you're looking for.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Select items">
    Check the items you want to restore. Multi-select works.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Restore">
    Items reappear in their original location — clients back to the Clients list, projects back under their client, media back to the Media library.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## What if the parent was deleted too?

* Restoring a **project** whose parent **client** is also archived — the client is restored first automatically. You end up with both the client and its project active again.
* Restoring a **project** whose parent client was **permanently deleted** — the project returns to the Archive with a new error state. You'll need to reassign it to a different active client.

## Retention

You can only restore items within the retention window (90 days by default). Past that, the item is permanently purged.


# View Archive
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/archive/view

Browse every soft-deleted client, project, and media item.

Everything you delete in Oviond is **soft-deleted first** — moved to the Archive rather than permanently removed. The Archive view shows:

* Archived **projects** (reports and dashboards)
* Archived **clients** (with their projects nested)
* Archived **media** (images and files)

From here you can **restore** an item back to its original location, or **permanently delete** it to free up quota. Items older than the retention window (90 days by default) are purged automatically.


# Authentication
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/authentication

Oviond authenticates API requests with Bearer tokens. Learn how to generate an API key, set the Authorization header, and handle auth errors.

Oviond's REST API uses Bearer token authentication. Every request must include your API key in the `Authorization` header. This page explains how to generate an API key from your account settings and how to pass it correctly in your requests.

## Generate an API key

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open API key settings">
    In the Oviond app, click your account name in the top-right corner and select **Settings**. Navigate to the **API Keys** tab.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Create a new key">
    Click **Create API Key**. Give it a descriptive name (for example, "Zapier integration" or "Custom dashboard") so you can identify it later.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Copy and store the key">
    Your key is shown once. Copy it and store it securely — you won't be able to see the full key again after you close this dialog.

    <Warning>
      Treat your API key like a password. Do not commit it to version control or expose it in client-side code.
    </Warning>
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Make authenticated requests

Include your API key in the `Authorization` header as a Bearer token on every request:

```bash theme={null}
curl -X GET https://api.oviond.com/v1/users/me \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY"
```

Replace `YOUR_API_KEY` with the key you generated above.

## Example: Fetch your account

```bash theme={null}
curl https://api.oviond.com/v1/users/me \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_API_KEY"
```

A successful response returns your user profile:

```json theme={null}
{
  "id": "usr_abc123",
  "email": "you@youragency.com",
  "name": "Your Name",
  "accountId": "acc_xyz789"
}
```

## Authentication errors

| Status             | Meaning                                                          |
| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `401 Unauthorized` | The `Authorization` header is missing or the key is invalid.     |
| `403 Forbidden`    | Your key is valid but doesn't have permission for this resource. |

If you receive a `401`, double-check that you've included the `Authorization: Bearer <key>` header and that the key hasn't been revoked.

## Manage your API keys

You can list, create, and revoke API keys at any time from **Settings → API Keys** in the app, or via the [API Keys API](/api-keys/list).

<Note>
  Each API key is scoped to your account. Keys created by team members operate within their permission level.
</Note>


# Add Automation
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/automations/add

Schedule a report or dashboard for recurring email delivery.

An **automation** sends a report to a list of recipients on a recurring schedule — daily, weekly, or monthly. Once configured, Oviond renders the report, attaches it (if you chose PDF), and emails it from the sender you specify.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Automations">
    From the left sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click + New Automation">
    Opens the create dialog.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick a project">
    Choose the report or dashboard to deliver.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set the schedule">
    Frequency (daily / weekly / monthly), day, time, and timezone. See [Scheduled Delivery](/automations/scheduled).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Configure the email">
    Recipients, subject, sender, and message body. See [Email Delivery](/automations/email).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose attachment">
    Inline HTML only, or attach a [PDF export](/automations/pdf).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Create">
    The automation is saved and starts firing on the next scheduled run.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Tip>
  Always [Test](/automations/test) a new automation before going live — the test send bypasses the schedule and emails you immediately, so you can verify the content looks right.
</Tip>


# Delete Automation
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/automations/delete

Permanently remove a scheduled delivery.

Deleting an automation removes the schedule and every run-history entry associated with it. The underlying **project** is not affected — only the scheduled delivery.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Automations screen">
    From the left sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the automation menu">
    Click the three-dot menu on the row.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Delete">
    Confirm when prompted.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Tip>
  If you're temporarily pausing a client's deliveries, toggle **Pause** on the automation instead of deleting. Paused automations keep their history and can be resumed any time. See [List Automations](/automations/list).
</Tip>

## Bulk delete

To remove multiple automations at once, enter **Select mode** on the Automations list, check each automation you want to remove, and click **Delete selected** from the action bar.


# Email Delivery
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/automations/email

Configure subject, sender, and body of automation emails.

By default, Oviond sends automated report emails from an Oviond address. You can change this to send from your own agency domain (e.g., `reports@youragency.com`) for a fully branded experience. Oviond supports two custom sending options: custom domain email and SMTP.

## Custom email domain

Send emails from your own domain using Oviond's email infrastructure. You'll need to add DNS records to verify ownership.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Email Settings">
    Go to **Settings → Email** and click **Add Sending Domain**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enter your domain">
    Type the domain you want to send from (e.g., `youragency.com`). Oviond will generate SPF and DKIM DNS records for you.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Add DNS records">
    Log in to your DNS provider and add the SPF and DKIM records that Oviond provides. The exact steps depend on your provider.

    <Note>
      DNS changes can take up to 24–48 hours to propagate. Oviond checks verification status automatically.
    </Note>
  </Step>

  <Step title="Verify your domain">
    Return to **Settings → Email** and click **Check Verification**. Once verified, you can set the sender name and email address (e.g., `Reports <reports@youragency.com>`).
  </Step>
</Steps>

## SMTP configuration

If you prefer to route emails through your own email provider (e.g., SendGrid, Mailgun, AWS SES), configure SMTP.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open SMTP settings">
    Go to **Settings → Email → SMTP** and toggle on **Use Custom SMTP**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enter SMTP credentials">
    Fill in the host, port, username, and password from your email provider. Common ports are `587` (TLS) and `465` (SSL).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Test the connection">
    Click **Test SMTP** to send a test message. Oviond confirms whether the connection succeeded.

    <Warning>
      If SMTP credentials are incorrect, automated report emails will fail to send. Always test before saving.
    </Warning>
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    Click **Save**. All subsequent report emails will route through your SMTP server.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Send a test email

At any time, go to **Settings → Email** and click **Send Test Email** to verify your current configuration sends correctly to an address you specify.


# Run History
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/automations/history

See every past run of an automation and its status.

The **Run History** page lists every scheduled delivery an automation has made — successful or not — along with the exact time, recipient list, and any error if it failed.

## Status codes

| Status        | Meaning                                                                          |
| ------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Delivered** | Email was successfully queued to every recipient's provider.                     |
| **Partial**   | Some recipients received it, others bounced or errored.                          |
| **Failed**    | The automation errored before sending — typically a rendering or template issue. |
| **Skipped**   | Automation was paused when the scheduled run fired.                              |

## When to check History

* **Debugging a missed delivery** — a client says they didn't receive the report; check History to confirm it fired and to see the recipient-level delivery status.
* **Auditing** — prove that an end-of-month report was delivered on time.
* **Error triage** — a row showing **Failed** includes the specific error (e.g. *Integration error — Google Ads token expired*).

## Retention

Run history is retained for 12 months. Older entries are purged automatically.


# List Automations
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/automations/list

View, pause, or edit every scheduled delivery.

The **Automations** screen lists every scheduled delivery in your account, grouped by project. Each row shows the automation's name, schedule, next run time, last-run status, and a pause toggle.

## Row actions

| Action              | Result                                                                            |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Pause / Unpause** | Toggles whether the automation fires on its next scheduled run. See below.        |
| **Edit**            | Opens the automation for editing (schedule, recipients, subject, body).           |
| **Test**            | Sends the automation immediately — see [Test Automation](/automations/test).      |
| **History**         | Opens the run log — see [Run History](/automations/history).                      |
| **Delete**          | Permanently removes the automation. See [Delete Automation](/automations/delete). |

## Pause / Unpause

Paused automations stay in the list but skip their scheduled runs. Paused is a soft state — toggle it back on any time to resume. Useful when a client is on hold, or you're switching report templates mid-month.

## Filters

* **Project** — show only automations for a single project.
* **Status** — filter to active, paused, or last-run-failed.


# PDF Export
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/automations/pdf

Generate a PDF from a report — on demand or attached to an automation.

Oviond can render any report as a PDF — either exported on demand from the report view, or delivered automatically as an email attachment via an automation. PDF generation runs asynchronously in the background, so large reports don't block your browser.

## On-demand PDF export

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the report">
    Navigate to the project you want to export.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Export">
    In the top toolbar, click **Export → PDF**. Oviond queues the PDF generation job.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Download">
    When the PDF is ready, Oviond notifies you with a download link. Generation typically takes 15–60 seconds depending on report size.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## PDF settings

Configure PDF layout per project in **Project Settings → PDF**:

| Setting              | Options             |
| -------------------- | ------------------- |
| Page size            | A4, Letter, Legal   |
| Orientation          | Portrait, Landscape |
| Cover page           | On / Off            |
| Include page numbers | On / Off            |

<Tip>
  Landscape orientation works best for wide tables and multi-column chart layouts.
</Tip>

## Automated PDF delivery

To send PDFs on a recurring schedule, create an automation and select **PDF attachment** as the delivery method. See [Scheduled Reports](/automations/scheduled) for the full setup steps.

## Troubleshooting

**Widgets appear cut off** — Switch to landscape orientation or reduce the number of columns in your layout.

**Images or logos missing** — Ensure all image URLs in your report are publicly accessible. Private or expired URLs won't render in PDF.

**PDF generation times out** — Very large reports with many widgets can occasionally time out. Try splitting the report across multiple pages.


# Scheduled Delivery
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/automations/scheduled

Set the cadence, day, and time an automation fires.

Oviond automations let you schedule reports to be delivered automatically to your clients — no manual sending required. You can send reports as live links or PDF attachments on any recurring schedule you define.

## Create an automation

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Automations">
    In the left sidebar, click **Automations**, then click **New Automation**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Select a report">
    Choose the project (report) you want to send. You can only select one project per automation.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set the schedule">
    Choose a frequency: **Daily**, **Weekly**, or **Monthly**. Then set the specific day (for weekly/monthly), time, and timezone for delivery.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Configure delivery">
    Select the delivery method:

    * **Email link** — sends a live link to the report
    * **PDF attachment** — attaches a rendered PDF to the email

    Add recipient email addresses, customize the email subject line, and optionally write a message body.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save the automation">
    Click **Save**. The automation is active immediately and will run on the next scheduled time.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Manage automations

From the **Automations** list you can:

* **Pause / unpause** — Click the toggle next to any automation to suspend it without deleting it.
* **Edit** — Update the schedule, recipients, or delivery settings at any time.
* **Delete** — Remove automations individually or in bulk using the checkboxes.

## View execution history

Click on any automation to open its detail view and see a log of every past send — including the timestamp and delivery status (success or failure). This is useful for confirming reports were delivered or diagnosing missed sends.

## Test an automation

To send an immediate test delivery without waiting for the next scheduled time, open the automation detail and click **Send Now**. The report is sent to all configured recipients right away.

<Note>
  Test sends count toward your monthly email quota.
</Note>


# Test Automation
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/automations/test

Send an automation immediately to verify its content.

A **test send** bypasses the schedule and emails the automation to you (or a test recipient) right now. Use it after creating or editing an automation — it's the fastest way to catch a broken widget, wrong logo, or mangled subject line before the real delivery.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the automation">
    From the Automations list, click the automation's row or the three-dot menu.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Test Send">
    Oviond renders the report with the current settings and sends it exactly as the scheduled delivery would.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Optionally override recipient">
    By default the test goes to your logged-in email. Use the **Send to** field to target a different address.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Check the inbox">
    Verify the subject, sender name, body copy, and — if attached — the PDF looks correct.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  Test sends don't count against your monthly email quota and don't appear in [Run History](/automations/history) — they're out-of-band.
</Note>


# Invoices
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/billing/invoices

Download every past invoice for your Oviond subscription.

Oviond issues an invoice every time your plan renews. Download any past invoice for your accounting or expense records.

## View and download

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Billing">
    From **Account → Billing → Invoices**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Find the invoice">
    Invoices are listed most-recent first. Each row shows the date, amount, and payment status.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Download the PDF">
    Click **Download** on any row — the invoice downloads as a PDF you can email or file.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## What's on an invoice

* Your agency name and billing address (from [Company](/company/overview))
* The Oviond plan charged
* Any taxes collected for your region
* Payment method used (last 4 digits of the card)

<Note>
  If you need an invoice addressed to a different entity or with a custom tax ID, update your billing details via the [Subscription](/billing/subscription) portal before the next renewal. Past invoices can't be re-issued retroactively.
</Note>


# Plans
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/billing/plans

Compare Oviond subscription plans and what's included at each tier.

Oviond offers subscription plans designed for agencies of different sizes. Each plan defines how many clients, team members, and integrations you can use. You can view and compare plans directly from your account at any time.

## View available plans

Go to **Settings → Billing → Plans** to see all available subscription tiers, their pricing, and what's included at each level.

Each plan specifies:

| Limit            | What it means                                           |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Clients**      | Maximum number of client workspaces you can create      |
| **Team members** | Seats available for colleagues on your account          |
| **Integrations** | Total connected data source accounts across all clients |
| **Projects**     | Maximum number of reports/dashboards                    |
| **Automations**  | Number of scheduled automations                         |

## Upgrade or change plans

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Billing">
    Go to **Settings → Billing** and click **Change Plan**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Select a plan">
    Review the plans and click **Select** next to the one you want.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Complete checkout">
    You'll be taken to a Stripe-hosted checkout page. Enter your payment details and confirm.
  </Step>
</Steps>

Upgrades take effect immediately. Downgrades apply at the end of your current billing period.

<Note>
  If you need more clients or seats than any listed plan offers, contact Oviond support to ask about enterprise options.
</Note>

## Lifetime deal (LTD) add-ons

If you purchased a lifetime deal through a partner promotion, you can apply add-ons from **Settings → Billing → Lifetime Deal**. Enter your LTD code to unlock the additional capacity.


# Subscription
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/billing/subscription

Change plan, update payment method, or cancel your Oviond subscription.

Oviond uses Stripe to handle all subscription billing. You can manage your plan, payment method, and invoices directly from your account settings or through the Stripe customer portal.

## Access the billing portal

Go to **Settings → Billing** and click **Manage Billing**. This opens the Stripe customer portal in a new tab, where you can:

* Update your credit card or payment method
* View and download past invoices
* Cancel or change your subscription

## Update your payment method

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the billing portal">
    Go to **Settings → Billing → Manage Billing**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Payment Methods">
    In the Stripe portal, select **Payment Methods** and click **Add payment method**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enter card details">
    Enter your new card number, expiry, and CVC, then click **Add**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set as default">
    Click **Set as default** next to your new card to use it for future charges.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## View and download invoices

In the Stripe billing portal, click **Invoice history** to see all past payments. Click any invoice to view or download a PDF receipt.

## Cancel your subscription

In the Stripe billing portal, click **Cancel plan**. Your subscription remains active until the end of the current billing period. After that, your account switches to a limited free tier.

<Warning>
  Canceling your subscription does not delete your data immediately. However, if your usage exceeds free-tier limits, some features will become inaccessible until you resubscribe.
</Warning>


# April 2026
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/changelog/2026-04

Release notes for the Oviond REST API.

<Update label="2026-04-20" description="Initial release">
  ### Public API reference launched

  The Oviond REST API reference is now live on Mintlify. The full endpoint catalog — projects, widgets, integrations, automations, public share links — is auto-generated from the backend's OpenAPI spec and kept in sync on every release.

  * Interactive **Try it** console against `https://api.oviond.com`
  * Bearer-token authentication via API keys generated in **Settings → API Keys**
  * Grouped by domain: Account, Projects, Widgets, Integrations, Automations, Public, and more
</Update>

<Update label="2026-04-19" description="Automations">
  ### Scheduled report delivery

  Schedule reports to be emailed to clients on a recurring cadence. Automations support pause/unpause, run history, and on-demand test sends.

  * `POST /v1/automations` — create an automation
  * `POST /v1/automations/:id/pause` and `/unpause`
  * `POST /v1/automations/:id/test` — send a test email without logging a run
  * `GET /v1/automations/history` — inspect past deliveries
</Update>

<Update label="2026-04-18" description="PDF export">
  ### PDF generation

  Render any report to PDF with selectable format and orientation. Jobs run asynchronously — enqueue, then poll for status until the download URL is ready.

  * `POST /v1/pdf/generate` — enqueue a job
  * `GET /v1/pdf/status/:job_id` — poll until complete
</Update>

<Update label="2026-04-17" description="White label & custom domains">
  ### Fully branded client experiences

  Serve reports under your own domain with end-to-end white label — logo, favicon, colors, OG preview, password gating, and error messaging.

  * `PUT /v1/white-label` — update branding settings
  * `POST /v1/white-label/domains` — add a custom domain (auto-detects Cloudflare / GoDaddy)
  * `GET /v1/white-label/domains/:id/status` — check DNS verification via Vercel
</Update>

<Update label="2026-04-16" description="Templates & themes">
  ### Reusable report scaffolds

  Create templates from existing reports (pages + widgets + date settings carry over) and apply themes to control typography, color palette, and chart styling across any project.

  * `POST /v1/templates` — create a template, optionally copying from a project
  * `POST /v1/themes` — create a theme (chart colors auto-derive from `primary_color`)
  * Shared library templates available to every account
</Update>

<Update label="2026-04-15" description="Media library">
  ### Uploads, folders, and S3-backed storage

  Upload images and assets, organize them into folders, and reference them directly from widgets. Deletes are soft — files stay in S3 for recovery.

  * `POST /v1/media/upload-url` — request a pre-signed upload URL
  * `GET /v1/media` and `/v1/media/folders`
  * Bulk delete, rename, and folder management
</Update>

<Update label="2026-04-14" description="API keys">
  ### Programmatic access

  Issue API keys from **Settings → API Keys** to call the REST API outside the app. Keys can be revoked instantly and support role-based scopes.

  * `POST /v1/api-keys` — create a key (secret shown once)
  * `POST /v1/api-keys/:id/revoke`
</Update>

<Update label="2026-04-13" description="Alerts">
  ### Workspace-level notifications

  In-app alerts for automation failures, integration disconnects, and team events — with read/unread tracking and per-user delivery preferences.

  * `GET /v1/alerts` — list recent alerts
  * `GET /v1/alerts/unread-count`
  * `PUT /v1/alerts/preferences` — opt in/out of email delivery
</Update>

<Update label="2026-04-12" description="Calculated & saved metrics">
  ### Custom KPIs and reusable queries

  Define formulas that combine metrics across integrations (e.g. `Revenue / Sessions`), and save data-query configurations you use across multiple reports.

  * `POST /v1/calculated-metrics` — define a formula
  * `POST /v1/saved-metrics` — save a query config
  * Both support duplicate for quick variants
</Update>

<Update label="2026-04-11" description="Custom data">
  ### Bring your own CSVs

  Upload CSV datasets and query them alongside integration data. Useful for offline sales, forecasts, or any metric that doesn't live in a connected platform.

  * `POST /v1/custom-data` — upload and parse a dataset
  * `POST /v1/custom-data/:id/duplicate`
</Update>

<Update label="2026-04-10" description="Integrations">
  ### Connect marketing platforms via OAuth

  OAuth 2.1 connection flow for Google Analytics, Meta Ads, LinkedIn, and the rest of the Oviond integration catalog. Datasources link to clients; profiles are selected per integration.

  * `GET /v1/integrations/auth-url` — start an OAuth flow
  * `POST /v1/integrations/auth` — exchange the code
  * `POST /v1/data/query` — pull metrics from connected sources
</Update>

<Update label="2026-04-09" description="Reports, pages & widgets">
  ### The report canvas

  The core editor API — create reports, add pages, and drop widgets (charts, KPIs, text, images, embeds, goals, buttons). Atomic `/save` endpoint persists pages and bulk widget upserts in a single call.

  * `POST /v1/projects` — create a report or dashboard
  * `POST /v1/pages` — add a page to a project or template
  * `POST /v1/widgets` — batch upsert widgets
</Update>

<Update label="2026-04-08" description="Clients & folders">
  ### Client workspaces

  Every report ties to a client. Organize clients into folders, bulk-delete, and filter by folder across the dashboard.

  * `POST /v1/clients` — create a client
  * `POST /v1/folders` — organize clients into folders
  * `PUT /v1/clients/:id/move` — move between folders
</Update>

<Update label="2026-04-07" description="Team & account management">
  ### Multi-user accounts

  Invite team members, assign roles, and restrict users to a subset of projects. Account-level settings for company info, branding, and billing.

  * `POST /v1/users/invite` — invite a team member
  * `GET /v1/account` and `/v1/account/usage` — plan + usage snapshot
  * `PUT /v1/branding` — set logo, favicon, brand color
</Update>

<Update label="2026-04-06" description="Public sharing">
  ### Shareable report links

  Every project gets a short `nano_id` share URL. Public endpoints return the full rendered payload — widgets, branding, theme, white label — in a single request, with no authentication required.

  * `GET /v1/public/resolve/:nano_id` — resolve a short link
  * `GET /v1/public/report/:account_id/:client_id/:project_id` — full render payload
  * `GET /v1/public/og-meta/:nano_id` — OG tags for link previews
</Update>


# Create a Client
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/clients/add

Add a new client workspace in a few fields.

From the Clients list, click **+ Add Client**. Only **Name** is required — the rest can be filled in later.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Name">
    The client's name appears in the sidebar, on report headers, and in the client picker. You can rename it later without affecting existing reports.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Website (optional)">
    When you add a website, Oviond automatically fetches a screenshot and uses it as the client's card thumbnail. You can refresh the screenshot from the client's settings.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Timezone & currency (optional)">
    Sets the default locale for every report under this client — dates, scheduled delivery windows, and money widgets all respect these values.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Manager (optional)">
    Assign a team member as the primary contact. Useful for filtering activity and routing alerts.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Theme & folder (optional)">
    Pick a default [Theme](/themes/list) for new projects, and drop the client into a [Folder](/clients/folders) to organize it with related clients.
  </Step>
</Steps>

Once saved, the client has an empty workspace. Next steps are usually:

1. [Connect a Data Source](/data-sources/connect) for this client
2. [Create your first Project](/projects/list) — a Report or Dashboard


# Archive Client
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/clients/archive

Soft-delete a client with full recovery within the retention window.

Archiving a client removes it from your active dashboard without permanently destroying anything. The client's projects, pages, widgets, and data connections are preserved and can be restored at any time before the retention window expires.

## Archive a client

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the client's menu">
    From the client card or inside the client's workspace, click the three-dot menu.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Archive">
    Click **Archive client**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Confirm">
    The client disappears from the active list and moves to the [Archive](/archive/view).
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Restore an archived client

From the [Archive](/archive/view) view, find the client, click **Restore**, and the client reappears in your active list with all its projects intact.

## Retention

Archived clients older than the retention window (90 days by default) are **permanently deleted** by a background job. If you need to keep a client indefinitely without it cluttering the active dashboard, keep it in a dedicated [folder](/clients/folders) instead.


# Bulk Delete
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/clients/bulk-delete

Remove multiple clients at once from your workspace.

Bulk delete is the fastest way to clean up after offboarding several clients at the same time — or to clear out test clients you no longer need.

<Warning>
  Bulk-deleted clients move to the [Archive](/archive/view) along with all their projects, dashboards, and data-source connections. The clients can be restored from the Archive within the retention window, but anything permanently deleted from the Archive is unrecoverable. Export what you need to keep first.
</Warning>

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the client list">
    Go to your home dashboard.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enable multi-select">
    Hover a client card and click the checkbox that appears, or toggle **Select** mode from the dashboard toolbar. Check every client you want to remove.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Delete">
    With clients selected, click **Delete** in the action bar at the top of the list.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Confirm">
    Review the list in the confirmation dialog, then click **Confirm delete**. Selected clients move to the Archive.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Undo a bulk delete

Go to [Archive](/archive/view), select the clients you just removed, and click **Restore**. Their projects and data connections come back intact.


# Group Clients
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/clients/folders

Group clients into folders to keep a large client list navigable.

A **folder** is an organizational bucket that groups clients together. Folders appear in the sidebar and on your dashboard so you can jump straight to a subset of clients without scrolling.

Common patterns: grouping by industry (**E-commerce**, **Healthcare**), region (**North America**, **EMEA**), or team (**Account Manager — Jane**).

<Note>
  Folders are purely organizational — they don't affect permissions, billing, or reports. A single client can be assigned to multiple folders if needed.
</Note>

## Create a folder

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the sidebar">
    In the Clients sidebar, click **New folder**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Name the folder">
    Enter a name (for example, **Retail Clients**).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    The folder appears in the sidebar and on your dashboard, ready to receive clients.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Assign clients to a folder

From the client's three-dot menu, choose **Move to folder** and pick the target. You can also drag a client card onto a folder in the sidebar.

## Rename or delete a folder

Click the three-dot menu next to a folder name to **Rename** or **Delete** it. Deleting a folder does **not** delete the clients inside — they return to the main unorganized list.


# List Clients
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/clients/list

View and filter every client in your workspace.

The **Clients** list is your home dashboard — every client you've created appears here, most-recently-active first. Each client card shows the client's name, website screenshot, project count, and folder (if assigned).

## Views

* **All Clients** — the unfiltered list.
* **By Folder** — narrow the list to a single folder from the sidebar.
* **Search** — type in the search bar to match by client name.
* **Sort** — switch between recent activity, name (A–Z), and date created.

## What's on a client card

| Element           | Description                                                                                                  |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Name**          | Shown in the card header and the sidebar.                                                                    |
| **Screenshot**    | Auto-generated from the client's website. Click **Refresh Screenshot** from the client's menu to regenerate. |
| **Project count** | Number of active reports and dashboards in the client's workspace.                                           |
| **Folder badge**  | The folder the client is assigned to, if any.                                                                |

<Note>
  The Clients list respects your **allowed projects** if your role is limited. Team members with restricted access only see clients whose projects they're authorized to view.
</Note>

## Quick actions

From any client card's three-dot menu:

* **Open** — enter the client's workspace.
* **Rename** — edit the client's display name inline.
* **Move to folder** — see [Group Clients](/clients/folders).
* **Archive** — soft-delete the client; see [Archive Client](/clients/archive).


# Customize Report Branding and Appearance
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/company/branding

Apply your agency's color palette, fonts, and logo to all client-facing Oviond reports. Use the live preview mode to review changes before saving.

Branding settings control the visual appearance of client-facing reports — the colors, fonts, and logo your clients see when they open a shared report link. These settings apply to the presentation layer of the report itself, separate from your agency identity settings in white labeling.

## White-label settings vs. branding settings

Oviond has two distinct layers of brand control:

| Setting                  | What it controls                                                                                                               |
| ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **White-label settings** | Your agency identity in the report frame — name, logo in the header, custom domain, email sender name. These are account-wide. |
| **Branding settings**    | The visual design of client-facing report content — the color palette, fonts, and logo that appear inside reports.             |

Configure white-label settings once to establish your agency's identity across all shared reports. Use branding settings to define the visual style of report content — either as a default for all reports or as a baseline your per-report themes build on. See [White-Label Reporting for Your Agency](/custom-domains/list) for the account-level identity settings.

## Open the branding settings panel

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open your account settings">
    Click your avatar or initials in the bottom-left corner of the sidebar, then select **Account Settings**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Select the Branding tab">
    In the account settings panel, click the **Branding** tab. This panel contains all visual design options for client-facing reports.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Customize the color palette

The color palette you set here applies to client-facing report views. It determines the primary accent color, supporting accent, and background used throughout reports.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Branding panel">
    Go to **Account Settings** > **Branding**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set the primary color">
    Click the **Primary color** swatch and enter a hex value or use the color picker. The primary color appears in headers, buttons, and dominant UI elements in client-facing report views.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set the accent color">
    Click the **Accent color** swatch and enter a hex value. The accent color is used for highlights and secondary interactive elements.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set the background color">
    Click the **Background color** swatch and enter a hex value. This sets the base background of the client-facing report view.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Preview your changes">
    Use the live preview panel on the right to see how your color choices look in a sample report view before saving.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save your changes">
    Click **Save** to apply the palette across all client-facing reports.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Tip>
  Match these colors to your agency's brand guide. Consistent color usage across reports, proposals, and your website reinforces your agency's professionalism to every client.
</Tip>

## Set a report-level logo

The branding settings panel includes a separate logo upload for report content — distinct from the header logo you set in white-label settings. This logo can appear as a watermark or header element within the report pages themselves.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Branding panel">
    Go to **Account Settings** > **Branding**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Upload the logo">
    Under **Report logo**, click **Upload** and select your image file. PNG or SVG with a transparent background works best.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Adjust positioning (if available)">
    If a positioning option is shown, choose where the logo appears within the report layout.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save your changes">
    Click **Save**.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  The report logo is separate from the logo in your white-label header settings. The white-label logo appears in the report frame (the outer chrome). The report logo appears inside the report content area itself.
</Note>

## Set fonts for reports

Font settings control the typefaces used for headings and body text inside client-facing report views.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Branding panel">
    Go to **Account Settings** > **Branding**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose a heading font">
    Use the **Heading font** dropdown to select the typeface for titles and section headers inside reports.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose a body font">
    Use the **Body font** dropdown to select the typeface for all other text — labels, descriptions, and widget text.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Preview your changes">
    Check the preview panel to see how the font pairing looks in context.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save your changes">
    Click **Save**.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Preview mode

Before saving any branding change, use the built-in preview to see how the update will look in a real report context.

The preview panel displays a sample report view using your current settings. Changes you make in the branding panel are reflected in the preview in real time. Save only when you're satisfied with the result — unsaved changes do not affect live client-facing reports.

## How branding settings interact with per-report themes

Branding settings establish the default visual style for all client-facing reports. Per-report themes, configured at the project level, can override these defaults for individual reports.

| Level                 | Scope                  | Overrides                                    |
| --------------------- | ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| **Branding settings** | All reports by default | —                                            |
| **Per-report theme**  | A single project       | Overrides branding defaults for that project |

If a project has a theme applied, that theme's colors and fonts take effect for that project's client-facing view. Projects without an applied theme fall back to your branding settings. See [Design Report Themes](/themes/list) for how to create and apply per-report themes.

<Tip>
  For most agencies, the best workflow is to set branding defaults that match your agency brand, then create per-client themes when a client's brand colors differ significantly from your own.
</Tip>


# Company
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/company/overview

Your agency's identity across every report and email.

The **Company** settings live at **Account → Company** and cover everything that represents your agency to clients:

* **Company name** — used in report headers and email footers
* **Logo & favicon** — applied to every report and the sign-in page on custom domains
* **Brand color palette** — generates chart color tokens used across dashboards
* **Support email** — the "reply-to" address shown on delivered reports

For white-label custom domains see **Custom Domains**. For per-report theming see **Templates → Themes**.


# Delete Custom Data
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/custom-data/delete

Permanently remove an uploaded CSV dataset.
