Deleting a dataset removes its rows and the dataset metadata. Widgets that referenced the dataset show *Data source unavailable* until re-wired to a different source.

<Warning>
  Deletion is permanent — there's no Archive recovery for Custom Data. Download the CSV back from the dataset preview first if you want a backup.
</Warning>

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Custom Data">
    From the sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the dataset's menu">
    Click the three-dot menu.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Delete">
    Confirm when prompted.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## If the dataset is in use

Oviond warns you if the dataset is referenced by any active widget before deletion. Review the list of dependent widgets — you may want to swap their data source first rather than leaving broken widgets.


# Duplicate Custom Data
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/custom-data/duplicate

Copy a dataset under a new name.

Duplicating clones a Custom Data dataset — rows, columns, and metadata — under a new name. Useful for:

* Creating a variant with different filtering or data transformations applied downstream
* Forking a dataset before a risky update, so you can compare side-by-side
* Spinning up test data from a verified production dataset

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Custom Data">
    From the sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the dataset's menu">
    Click the three-dot menu on the row.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Duplicate">
    The copy appears in the list as `Copy of [original]`.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Rename">
    Open the copy and give it a meaningful name.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  Widgets continue pointing at the original dataset — duplicating doesn't re-wire anything. If you want widgets to use the new dataset, open each widget and update its data source.
</Note>


# List Custom Data
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/custom-data/list

Every CSV dataset uploaded to your account.

The **Custom Data** list shows every dataset you've uploaded — name, row count, column count, file size, and upload date.

## Filters

* **Search** — match by dataset name.
* **Sort** — by name, upload date, or row count.

## What's on a row

| Column       | Description                                   |
| ------------ | --------------------------------------------- |
| **Name**     | The label you gave the dataset on upload.     |
| **Columns**  | Number of columns parsed from the CSV header. |
| **Rows**     | Number of data rows (excluding the header).   |
| **Size**     | Raw file size of the CSV.                     |
| **Uploaded** | Timestamp of the most recent upload.          |

## Quick actions

From any row's three-dot menu:

* **Preview** — see the first 50 rows.
* **Update** — see [Update Custom Data](/custom-data/update).
* **Duplicate** — see [Duplicate Custom Data](/custom-data/duplicate).
* **Delete** — see [Delete Custom Data](/custom-data/delete).


# Update Custom Data
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/custom-data/update

Refresh a dataset by re-uploading its CSV.

Updating replaces the dataset's rows with a new CSV. The dataset's ID and widget bindings stay intact — every widget that references the dataset re-renders with the new data.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the dataset">
    Click the dataset name in the Custom Data list.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Re-upload CSV">
    Opens the file picker.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Select the new CSV">
    Oviond parses it and replaces the existing rows.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    Widgets using this dataset pick up the new data on next render.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  The new CSV should use the **same column structure** as the original. If you add or rename columns, widgets configured against the old column names will need to be re-wired.
</Note>

## Rename without re-uploading

To just change the dataset's display name (leaving the rows intact), use **Rename** from the row's menu in the [List Custom Data](/custom-data/list) view.


# Upload Custom Data
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/custom-data/upload

Upload a CSV dataset to report on data that doesn't live in a connected integration.

**Custom Data** lets you upload your own CSV files and use them alongside data from connected integrations. Useful for:

* Offline sales, lead lists, or CRM exports
* Revenue forecasts and targets
* Any KPI that lives outside the integrations we support natively

## How it works

<Steps>
  <Step title="Upload a CSV">
    From **Data → Custom Data**, click **Upload** and pick a CSV. Oviond parses the headers into columns and stores the rows.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Reference it in a widget">
    In any widget, pick **Custom Data** as the datasource, then select your uploaded dataset and the column you want to visualize.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Update the data">
    Re-upload a CSV with the same structure to refresh the underlying rows — all widgets that reference the dataset update automatically.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  Custom Data is account-level — any client's project can reference any custom dataset you've uploaded. Combine it with integration data in the same widget using **Calculated Metrics**.
</Note>

## When to use Custom Data vs a Data Source

* **Data Sources** (Google Ads, Facebook, etc.) — live data pulled from a connected marketing platform.
* **Custom Data** — static or periodically-refreshed data you upload yourself.


# Add Domain
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/custom-domains/add

Register a custom domain so shared reports load under your agency's URL.

Custom domains replace `app.oviond.com` with your own URL on shared report links — e.g. `reports.youragency.com`. Oviond detects the DNS provider and tells you exactly which records to add.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Custom Domains">
    From **Account → Custom Domains**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click + Add Domain">
    Enter the subdomain you want to use — e.g. `reports.youragency.com`.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Detect DNS provider">
    Oviond auto-detects the domain's DNS host (Cloudflare, GoDaddy, Route 53, etc).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Copy the DNS records">
    Oviond shows a CNAME (and verification TXT) record. Add them exactly as shown in your DNS provider's admin.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Start verification">
    Click **Verify**. Oviond polls DNS until the records propagate.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Supported providers with auto-setup

If your domain is on **Cloudflare** or **GoDaddy**, Oviond can configure the DNS records for you via OAuth — you grant permission once and Oviond writes the records automatically. For other providers, add the records manually as shown in the UI.

See [Verify Domain](/custom-domains/verify) for the verification flow.


# Delete Domain
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/custom-domains/delete

Remove a custom domain from your account.

Deleting a domain stops it from serving reports and removes it from Vercel on Oviond's side. Shared links that used the custom domain will fall back to `app.oviond.com`.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Custom Domains">
    From **Account → Custom Domains**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the domain's menu">
    Click the three-dot menu.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Delete">
    Confirm when prompted.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Remove DNS records">
    Optionally clean up the CNAME/TXT records in your DNS provider — Oviond doesn't touch your DNS admin.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  Existing shared links keep working — they transparently redirect from `yourdomain.com` to `app.oviond.com`. If you want a hard cut-off, also remove the DNS records so the domain no longer resolves.
</Note>


# List Domains
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/custom-domains/list

Every custom domain registered to your Oviond account.

By default, shared report links use an `oviond.com` subdomain. A custom domain replaces that with a URL you own — for example, `reports.youragency.com`. Clients see only your domain, reinforcing your agency brand at every touchpoint.

## Before you start

To add a custom domain, you need:

* **A domain you own** — this can be a subdomain of your main domain (recommended) or a standalone domain.
* **Access to your domain's DNS settings** — you'll need to add a CNAME record. This is done through your domain registrar or DNS provider (for example, Cloudflare, GoDaddy, or Namecheap).

<Note>
  DNS changes can take up to 48 hours to propagate globally, though most providers update within a few hours. Plan ahead if you have a deadline for switching clients to the new domain.
</Note>

## Add a custom domain

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open White Label settings">
    Go to **Account Settings** > **White Label**, then scroll to the **Custom Domain** section.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enter your domain">
    In the **Custom domain** field, type the domain or subdomain you want to use — for example, `reports.youragency.com`. Do not include `https://`.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save to generate your CNAME record">
    Click **Save** (or **Add domain**). Oviond generates a CNAME record specific to your account. The target value is displayed on screen — copy it before leaving this page.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Add the CNAME record to your DNS provider

Once Oviond gives you the CNAME target, you need to add it to your DNS provider. How you do this depends on which provider you use.

### Cloudflare and GoDaddy (automated setup)

Oviond offers automated DNS setup for Cloudflare and GoDaddy. If you use either of these providers:

<Steps>
  <Step title="Select your DNS provider">
    In the **Custom Domain** section, choose **Cloudflare** or **GoDaddy** from the provider selector.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Authenticate with your provider">
    Follow the prompts to connect your Cloudflare or GoDaddy account. Oviond requests permission to add the DNS record on your behalf.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Confirm the record">
    Review the CNAME record Oviond will add, then confirm. Oviond adds the record automatically and begins verification.
  </Step>
</Steps>

### Other DNS providers (manual setup)

For all other providers, add the CNAME record manually through your DNS provider's control panel:

<Steps>
  <Step title="Log in to your DNS provider">
    Open your domain registrar or DNS provider and navigate to the DNS management panel for your domain.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Add a new CNAME record">
    Create a new record with the following values:

    | Field              | Value                                                                                                           |
    | ------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
    | **Type**           | CNAME                                                                                                           |
    | **Name / Host**    | The subdomain portion of your custom domain (for example, `reports` if your domain is `reports.youragency.com`) |
    | **Value / Target** | The CNAME target provided by Oviond                                                                             |
    | **TTL**            | 3600 (or your provider's default)                                                                               |
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save the record">
    Save the DNS record in your provider's panel. Changes begin propagating immediately but may take time to fully resolve.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Verify DNS propagation

After adding the CNAME record, return to Oviond and wait for verification.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Return to White Label settings">
    Go back to **Account Settings** > **White Label** > **Custom Domain**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Check the verification status">
    Oviond automatically checks whether the CNAME record resolves correctly. The status indicator shows one of:

    * **Pending** — the record hasn't propagated yet. Check back later.
    * **Verified** — DNS is configured correctly. Your custom domain is active.
    * **Error** — the record wasn't found or doesn't match. Double-check the CNAME target you entered.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Wait if needed">
    If the status is **Pending**, wait a few hours and check again. Full global propagation can take up to 48 hours. You can use a tool like [dnschecker.org](https://dnschecker.org) to monitor propagation status independently.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## After verification

Once Oviond verifies your domain:

* All new shared report links use your custom domain automatically.
* Existing report links on `oviond.com` continue to work during a transition period, but new links will use your domain.
* Clients who open a shared link see your domain in the browser address bar.

<Note>
  SSL is provisioned automatically for your custom domain. You don't need to set up a certificate manually.
</Note>

## Remove a custom domain

<Warning>
  Removing your custom domain immediately breaks any existing shared report links that use it. Clients who have bookmarked or saved those links will no longer be able to open them. Before removing a domain, notify affected clients or update the links you've shared with them.
</Warning>

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open White Label settings">
    Go to **Account Settings** > **White Label** > **Custom Domain**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Remove the domain">
    Click **Remove** next to your custom domain. Confirm when prompted.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Clean up your DNS records">
    Log in to your DNS provider and delete the CNAME record you added. Leaving an unused CNAME record in your DNS is harmless but unnecessary.
  </Step>
</Steps>

After removal, Oviond falls back to the default `oviond.com` domain for all shared report links.


# Verify Domain
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/custom-domains/verify

Confirm DNS records are live so your custom domain activates.

After you [add a domain](/custom-domains/add), Oviond watches DNS for the required records. Once they resolve correctly, the domain moves to **Active** and shared links start rendering under it.

## Statuses

| Status        | Meaning                                                       |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Pending**   | Just added. DNS records haven't propagated yet.               |
| **Verifying** | Records are visible but SSL is still provisioning.            |
| **Active**    | Records and SSL are live — the domain is fully functional.    |
| **Failed**    | Verification timed out. Records are missing or misconfigured. |

## Trigger a re-check

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Custom Domains">
    From **Account → Custom Domains**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click the domain">
    Opens the detail panel with the current DNS record status.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Verify">
    Oviond re-queries DNS and Vercel (the SSL provider) and updates the status immediately.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Typical DNS propagation times

* **Cloudflare / Vercel DNS** — under 1 minute
* **Route 53** — 1–5 minutes
* **GoDaddy, Namecheap** — 5–30 minutes
* **Older registrars** — up to 24 hours

If the domain stays in **Pending** after a few hours, double-check the CNAME and TXT records match exactly what Oviond showed when you added the domain.


# Connect
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/data-sources/connect

Authorize Oviond to pull data from Google Ads, Facebook, and 30+ other platforms.

Connecting a marketing platform to a client is a two-step process. First, you authorize the platform through OAuth — this creates an **auth profile** in your Oviond workspace. Second, you link a specific account or data source from that platform to the client. Once linked, that data source is available in widgets when you build reports for that client.

<Note>
  Oviond requests read-only access to all connected platforms. Oviond cannot make changes to your campaigns, budgets, or account settings.
</Note>

## Step 1: Authorize the platform

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Integrations">
    Click **Integrations** in the left sidebar. This shows all platforms available to connect.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Find the platform">
    Browse the list or use the search bar to find the platform you want to connect (for example, Google Ads or Facebook Ads).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Connect">
    Click **Connect** next to the platform name. Oviond opens an OAuth authorization window.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Log in and grant permissions">
    Log in to the platform using the account credentials for the client (or your agency's manager account). Review the permissions and click **Allow** or **Authorize** to grant Oviond read access.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Return to Oviond">
    After authorization, the platform redirects you back to Oviond. The new auth profile now appears under that platform in your integrations list.
  </Step>
</Steps>

If you already have an auth profile for this platform (for example, you previously connected Google Ads for another client), you can skip Step 1 and proceed directly to linking the data source in Step 2.

## Step 2: Link a data source to a client

After authorization, you link the specific account or property to a client.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Navigate to the client">
    Go to **Clients** in the left sidebar and open the client you want to connect data to.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the Integrations tab">
    Click the **Integrations** tab on the client's profile page.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Add a data source">
    Click **+ Add Data Source**. A panel opens listing the platforms you have authorized.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Select the platform and auth profile">
    Choose the platform (for example, Google Ads) and select the auth profile to use. If you have multiple auth profiles for the same platform, select the one that has access to this client's account.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Select the specific account">
    From the account dropdown, select the specific account, property, or sub-account to link to this client (for example, the Google Ads account for this particular client). Only accounts accessible under the selected auth profile appear in this list.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save the data source">
    Click **Save**. The data source is now linked to the client and available in report widgets.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Test the connection

After linking a data source, verify it is returning data correctly before building widgets.

1. On the client's **Integrations** tab, find the data source you just linked.
2. Click the three-dot menu next to it and select **Test Connection**.
3. Oviond attempts to fetch data from the platform. A success message confirms the connection is active.

If the test fails, see [Common connection issues](#common-connection-issues) below.

## Reconnect an expired or revoked integration

OAuth tokens expire or are revoked when you change your password, revoke app access from within the marketing platform, or the platform's token policy expires the session. When this happens, widgets connected to that integration stop displaying data and the integration shows a warning status.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Identify the expired integration">
    Go to **Integrations** in the left sidebar. Expired or revoked auth profiles display a warning icon or a **Reconnect** prompt.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Reconnect">
    Click **Reconnect** next to the affected auth profile.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Re-authorize through OAuth">
    Complete the OAuth flow again using the same account credentials. Once you authorize, Oviond updates the auth profile with a fresh token.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Verify the connection">
    After reconnecting, use **Test Connection** on the affected data sources to confirm data is flowing again.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  Reconnecting an auth profile restores all clients and widgets that used it — you do not need to re-link each client individually.
</Note>

## Common connection issues

<AccordionGroup>
  <Accordion title="Wrong account selected during OAuth">
    If you have multiple accounts on a platform (for example, both a personal Google account and an agency Google account), the OAuth window may default to the wrong one. To fix this:

    1. Go to **Integrations** and delete the incorrectly authorized auth profile.
    2. Click **Connect** again.
    3. In the OAuth window, click the account switcher or **Use a different account** and log in with the correct credentials.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Insufficient permissions">
    Oviond requires read access to the account you are connecting. If authorization succeeds but the connection test fails with a permissions error, verify that the account you used has at least read-level access in the marketing platform. For Google Ads, the account needs **Read only** access or higher. For Facebook Ads, the user must have the **Analyst** role or higher on the ad account.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Account not appearing in the data source list">
    If the account you expect does not appear after authorization:

    * Confirm the account is accessible under the login you used (for manager accounts, check that the sub-account is linked to your manager account).
    * Try disconnecting and re-authorizing the auth profile.
    * If using a Google Ads manager account, allow up to a few minutes for the full account hierarchy to load.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="Connection test passes but widgets show no data">
    A successful connection test confirms the authorization is valid, but data in widgets may still be empty if:

    * The date range on the widget is set to a period with no activity.
    * The specific campaign, property, or segment selected in the widget has no data for that period.
    * The account was created recently and has not yet accumulated data.

    Check the widget's date range and data source settings before troubleshooting the integration further.
  </Accordion>

  <Accordion title="OAuth popup is blocked">
    Some browsers block OAuth popups by default. If clicking **Connect** does nothing, check your browser's popup blocker and allow popups for your Oviond domain, then try again.
  </Accordion>
</AccordionGroup>


# Delete Data Source
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/data-sources/delete

Remove an OAuth connection from your account.

Deleting a data source revokes its OAuth token and removes it from the account — every client that had it linked loses access, and every widget referencing it shows *Data source unavailable* until re-wired.

<Warning>
  If the data source is currently feeding automations or active reports, deleting it will break them on the next render. Unlink it from clients first if you want a softer path — see [Link to Client](/data-sources/link).
</Warning>

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Data Sources">
    From the sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the source's menu">
    Click the three-dot menu on the integration card.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Delete">
    Confirm when prompted.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Optional: revoke in the source platform">
    Oviond revokes its own access token, but if you want to be extra-thorough, remove Oviond from the authorized-apps list in the source platform too (e.g. Google Account → Third-party apps).
  </Step>
</Steps>


# Link to Client
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/data-sources/link

Attach a connected data source to a specific client workspace.

A data source lives at the **account** level — one OAuth connection can be linked to many clients. Linking makes the source's profiles selectable inside widgets in that client's projects.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the client">
    From the Clients list.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the client's Data Sources tab">
    Inside the client workspace.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click + Link Data Source">
    Pick from the account-level data sources you've already connected.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick the profile for this client">
    Choose which ad account, property, or page maps to this client. See [Profiles](/data-sources/profiles).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    Every widget you build for this client can now pull from the linked data source + profile.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Unlink

From the client's Data Sources tab, click the three-dot menu next to a linked source and choose **Unlink**. The data source remains at the account level — it's just not available inside this specific client anymore. Widgets in this client's projects that referenced it will show *Data source unavailable*.


# List Data Sources
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/data-sources/list

Every marketing platform connected to your Oviond account.

Integrations are OAuth connections between Oviond and your clients' marketing platforms. Once connected, Oviond pulls live data from those platforms into your reports and dashboards — no manual exports or copy-pasting required. Each integration is connected on a per-client basis, so the data you see in a report always belongs to the client that report is built for.

## Supported integrations

Oviond supports 30+ marketing platforms across six categories.

| Category             | Platforms                                                                                         |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Paid Search**      | Google Ads, Microsoft Ads (Bing Ads)                                                              |
| **Paid Social**      | Facebook Ads, Instagram Ads, LinkedIn Ads, TikTok Ads, Pinterest Ads, Snapchat Ads, Twitter/X Ads |
| **Analytics**        | Google Analytics 4, Google Analytics (Universal), Adobe Analytics                                 |
| **SEO**              | Google Search Console, Semrush, Moz, Ahrefs                                                       |
| **Email Marketing**  | Mailchimp, Klaviyo, Campaign Monitor, ActiveCampaign                                              |
| **E-commerce & CRM** | Shopify, HubSpot, Salesforce                                                                      |

<Note>
  The full list of available integrations is visible inside Oviond under **Integrations** in the left sidebar. New platforms are added regularly.
</Note>

## How integrations work

When you connect an integration, Oviond guides you through an OAuth flow — you log in to the marketing platform and grant Oviond permission to read your data. Oviond stores the resulting authorization as an **auth profile**.

After authorization, you link a specific account or data source (for example, a particular Google Ads account or Facebook Ads campaign) to a client. That linked data source then becomes available in widgets when you build reports for that client.

### Auth profiles

An auth profile represents one authorized connection to a platform — typically tied to one login. If you manage multiple clients' accounts through a single agency login (such as a Google Ads manager account), one auth profile can cover all of those sub-accounts.

You can reuse the same auth profile across multiple clients. For example, if your agency has a single Google Ads manager account that contains sub-accounts for 10 clients, you authorize Google Ads once and then link each client to their respective sub-account — all without re-authenticating.

## Listing your connected integrations

To see all integrations connected across your workspace:

1. Click **Integrations** in the left sidebar.
2. The integrations list shows every platform you have authorized, along with the auth profiles and their connection status.

To see integrations for a specific client:

1. Navigate to the client from the **Clients** screen.
2. Open the client's **Integrations** tab. This lists only the data sources linked to that client.

## Testing an integration connection

If you want to verify that a connected integration is returning data correctly:

1. Go to **Integrations** and locate the integration you want to test.
2. Click the three-dot menu next to the integration.
3. Select **Test Connection**.

Oviond will attempt to fetch data from the platform and confirm whether the connection is active. If the test fails, see [Connect a Data Source to a Client](/data-sources/connect) for troubleshooting steps.

## Unlinking and deleting integrations

**Unlinking** removes the data source from a specific client but keeps the auth profile available for reuse. **Deleting** an auth profile removes it from your workspace entirely and disconnects all clients that relied on it.

### Unlink a data source from a client

1. Open the client and go to the **Integrations** tab.
2. Find the data source you want to remove.
3. Click the three-dot menu and select **Unlink**.

The auth profile remains available and can be relinked to the same or a different client at any time.

### Delete an auth profile

<Warning>
  Deleting an auth profile removes it permanently and disconnects every client and widget that used it. Any widgets in reports that relied on that connection will stop displaying data. This cannot be undone.
</Warning>

1. Click **Integrations** in the left sidebar.
2. Find the auth profile you want to delete.
3. Click the three-dot menu and select **Delete**.
4. Confirm the deletion in the dialog.

## Next steps

<CardGroup>
  <Card title="Connect a data source" icon="plug" href="/data-sources/connect">
    Step-by-step guide to authorizing a platform and linking it to a client.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Custom metrics and data" icon="sliders" href="/data-sources/metrics">
    Create calculated metrics, save metric presets, and upload custom CSV data.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>


# Metrics
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/data-sources/metrics

Saved and calculated metrics you can reuse across widgets.

Oviond gives you three tools for extending the default metrics that come with each integration: **calculated metrics** let you define your own formulas, **saved metrics** let you bookmark a metric configuration for quick reuse, and **custom data** lets you upload datasets that aren't available through any integration. Each tool is available from the **Metrics** section in the left sidebar.

***

## Calculated metrics

A calculated metric is a user-defined formula that combines one or more existing metrics into a new value. For example, you can create a click-through rate metric by dividing clicks by impressions and multiplying by 100, or a cost-per-lead metric by dividing spend by the number of leads. Calculated metrics appear alongside native metrics when you configure a widget.

### Create a calculated metric

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Metrics">
    Click **Metrics** in the left sidebar, then select the **Calculated Metrics** tab.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click New Calculated Metric">
    Click **+ New Calculated Metric**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Name the metric">
    Enter a name in the **Metric name** field. Choose a name that makes the formula's purpose clear (for example, "CTR %" or "Cost per Lead").
  </Step>

  <Step title="Write the formula">
    In the **Formula** field, build your calculation using the metric picker and standard operators (`+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, and parentheses for grouping). Start typing a metric name to search and insert it.

    Example formula for click-through rate:

    ```
    (Clicks / Impressions) * 100
    ```
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set the unit">
    Choose the unit that matches the output of your formula: **Number**, **Percentage**, or **Currency**. This controls how the value is formatted in widgets.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save the metric">
    Click **Save**. The calculated metric is now available across all your clients and reports.
  </Step>
</Steps>

### Edit a calculated metric

1. Go to **Metrics** > **Calculated Metrics**.
2. Find the metric you want to update and click the three-dot menu next to it.
3. Select **Edit**.
4. Update the name, formula, or unit as needed and click **Save**.

<Warning>
  Editing a calculated metric updates it everywhere it is used. Any widgets that display this metric will immediately reflect the new formula.
</Warning>

### Duplicate a calculated metric

1. Go to **Metrics** > **Calculated Metrics**.
2. Click the three-dot menu next to the metric and select **Duplicate**.
3. Oviond creates a copy prefixed with "Copy of". Open the copy, rename it, and adjust the formula as needed.

### Use a calculated metric in a widget

When adding or editing a widget in the report builder, open the **Metrics** selector. Your calculated metrics appear in the list alongside native metrics from connected integrations. Select the calculated metric as you would any other metric.

***

## Saved metrics

A saved metric is a bookmark for a specific metric configuration — including the metric itself, any filters applied, and the visualization type. Instead of re-configuring the same combination each time you add a widget, you save it once and select it from a list when building reports.

For example, you might save "Google Ads — Conversions (last 30 days, search campaigns only, displayed as KPI)" as a saved metric so you can drop it into any report in a few clicks.

### Create a saved metric

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Metrics">
    Click **Metrics** in the left sidebar, then select the **Saved Metrics** tab.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click New Saved Metric">
    Click **+ New Saved Metric**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose the metric">
    Select the platform, then choose the metric you want to save from the dropdown.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Apply filters">
    Add any filters you want to lock in — for example, filtering by campaign type, device, or date range. Filters set here are applied every time this saved metric is used in a widget.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose a visualization type">
    Select the default visualization for this metric: **KPI**, **Line chart**, **Bar chart**, or **Table**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Name and save">
    Enter a descriptive name and click **Save**.
  </Step>
</Steps>

### Use a saved metric when adding a widget

1. In the report builder, click **+ Add Widget**.
2. In the widget configuration panel, switch to the **Saved Metrics** tab.
3. Select the saved metric from the list. Oviond pre-fills the widget with the metric, filters, and visualization you defined.
4. Adjust any settings that need to differ for this specific widget, then click **Add Widget**.

***

## Custom data

Custom data lets you upload your own datasets into Oviond as data sources. This is useful for data that doesn't flow through any integration — such as offline conversions, manually tracked budget sheets, lead quality scores, or any other dataset your clients provide as a spreadsheet.

Each custom data source accepts up to **10,000 rows** per dataset. Once uploaded, a custom data source appears in the widget builder alongside your integration data, so you can visualize it in charts, tables, and KPI tiles.

### Upload a CSV as a custom data source

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Metrics">
    Click **Metrics** in the left sidebar, then select the **Custom Data** tab.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click New Custom Data">
    Click **+ New Custom Data**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Name the dataset">
    Enter a name that identifies the dataset (for example, "Offline Conversions — Q1 2025" or "Monthly Budget Sheet").
  </Step>

  <Step title="Upload your CSV">
    Click **Upload CSV** and select your file. The CSV must include a header row. Oviond reads the column names from the header and uses them as the available fields in the widget builder.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Map column types">
    For each column, confirm or set the data type: **Text**, **Number**, **Date**, or **Currency**. Setting the correct type ensures Oviond can aggregate and visualize the data properly.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save the data source">
    Click **Save**. The dataset is now available as a data source when building widgets.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  Custom data sources support up to 10,000 rows per dataset. If your CSV exceeds this limit, split it into multiple files and upload each as a separate data source.
</Note>

### Edit a custom data source

To update the data in an existing custom data source — for example, to add new rows for the current month:

1. Go to **Metrics** > **Custom Data** and find the dataset.
2. Click the three-dot menu and select **Edit**.
3. Upload a new CSV to replace the existing data, or edit the column type mappings.
4. Click **Save**. Widgets using this data source update automatically.

### Delete a custom data source

<Warning>
  Deleting a custom data source permanently removes the dataset and disconnects any widgets that use it. Those widgets will stop displaying data. This cannot be undone.
</Warning>

1. Go to **Metrics** > **Custom Data**.
2. Click the three-dot menu next to the dataset and select **Delete**.
3. Confirm the deletion.

### Use custom data in a widget

1. In the report builder, click **+ Add Widget**.
2. In the widget configuration panel, open the **Data Source** dropdown and select your custom data source from the list (it appears under the **Custom Data** section).
3. Choose the columns you want to display or aggregate.
4. Configure the visualization and click **Add Widget**.


# Profiles
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/data-sources/profiles

Pick which ad account, property, or page to report on for each connected data source.

A **profile** is the specific account, property, page, or view inside a data source. When you connect Google Ads you get access to every ad account the authorizing user can see — a profile is how you tell Oviond *which* ad account to report on per client.

## Why profiles matter

Without a profile selection, Oviond doesn't know which numbers to pull:

* **Google Ads** has a Customer ID per ad account
* **Google Analytics 4** has a Property per site
* **Facebook Ads** has an Ad Account per business
* **LinkedIn** has a Page per brand

Profiles resolve that ambiguity — each client's data source is anchored to one profile.

## Manage profiles

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Data Sources">
    From the sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick the data source">
    Click a connected integration to see its available profiles.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Select a profile">
    Click the profile the client uses. Widgets configured with this data source now pull from the selected profile.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Rename if needed">
    Give each profile a friendly label (e.g. *Acme — Main Site*) so your team picks the right one when building widgets.
  </Step>
</Steps>


# Test Connection
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/data-sources/test

Verify a data-source authorization is still valid.

Test Connection pings the data source's API with the stored OAuth token and reports back success or failure. Use it when:

* A widget shows *Integration error* and you want to check if re-auth is needed
* You just reconnected a data source and want to confirm it's working
* You're debugging a failed automation that depends on this data source

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Data Sources">
    From the sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the data source">
    Click the integration you want to test.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Test Connection">
    Oviond hits the provider's API using the stored token.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Read the result">
    **Success** — token is valid, data should flow. **Failed** — token expired or was revoked; reconnect.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## If the test fails

* Click **Reconnect** to kick off a fresh OAuth flow. The existing profile selections are preserved.
* If reconnect doesn't resolve it, the authorizing user's access in the source platform may have been revoked — check the source platform's admin for the token's status.


# Defaults
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/email-settings/defaults

Set the default subject, sender, and message for every automation email.

Defaults live at **Account → Email Settings → Defaults**. Anything you set here becomes the starting value every time you create a new [automation](/automations/add) — automations can override per-delivery.

## Fields

| Field                    | Default                   | Notes                                                                         |
| ------------------------ | ------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Default subject**      | `{project_name} — {date}` | Supports template variables like `{project_name}`, `{client_name}`, `{date}`. |
| **Default from name**    | Your agency company name  | Shown as the sender name in the recipient's inbox.                            |
| **Default from email**   | `hello@oviond.com`        | Override by adding a [sender](/email-settings/senders) on a verified domain.  |
| **Default message body** | Short Oviond-branded copy | Use the rich text editor to set your own intro copy.                          |

<Tip>
  Keeping the defaults client-agnostic (no hardcoded client names) means every automation inherits sensible defaults and you only customize the bits that differ per client.
</Tip>


# Email Domains
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/email-settings/domains

Verify domains so Oviond can send mail on their behalf.

An **email domain** is a domain authorized to send mail through Oviond. Verification involves adding DNS records (SPF, DKIM, DMARC) so recipient providers trust messages coming from your domain.

## Add a domain

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Email Settings → Domains">
    Under **Account**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click + Add Domain">
    Enter the domain — e.g. `youragency.com`.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Copy the DNS records">
    Oviond shows SPF, DKIM, and (optionally) DMARC records. Add them exactly as shown in your DNS provider.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Verify">
    Oviond queries DNS and marks the domain **Active** once records resolve.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Status

| Status      | Meaning                                                             |
| ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Pending** | DNS records not visible yet.                                        |
| **Active**  | All required records verified — senders under this domain can send. |
| **Failed**  | Verification timed out. Re-check records and click Verify again.    |

## Remove a domain

Deleting a domain immediately invalidates every [sender](/email-settings/senders) under it. Automations configured with those senders will fail on next fire — switch them to a different verified sender first.


# Email Logs
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/email-settings/logs

Every automation email Oviond has sent — status, events, and any errors.

The **Email Logs** view shows every individual email Oviond has sent — automation deliveries, test sends that hit recipients, and transactional emails (invites, password resets).

## Columns

| Column         | Description                                                   |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Recipient**  | The to-address that received the email.                       |
| **Subject**    | The subject line as rendered.                                 |
| **Sender**     | The from-address / sender name.                               |
| **Sent at**    | Timestamp of send.                                            |
| **Status**     | Delivered / Bounced / Deferred / Opened / Clicked.            |
| **Last event** | Most recent status change (via the provider's webhook).       |
| **Error**      | Present only for failed sends — the provider's error message. |

## Events

Provider webhook events update each log row in near-real-time:

* **Delivered** — accepted by the recipient's provider.
* **Bounced** — the recipient address rejected the message (hard bounce = invalid address).
* **Deferred** — temporary failure (mailbox full, greylisting) — usually retries.
* **Opened** / **Clicked** — tracked via pixel and link rewrites.

## Filters

* **Recipient search** — find every email sent to a specific address.
* **Date range** — narrow to a specific window.
* **Status** — show only bounced, deferred, or opened.

## Retention

Email logs are retained for 12 months.


# Senders
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/email-settings/senders

Add verified from-addresses for automation emails.

A **sender** is a specific from-address (e.g. `reports@youragency.com`) that Oviond can send automation emails from. Before a sender is usable, the domain it belongs to must be verified under [Email Domains](/email-settings/domains).

## Add a sender

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Email Settings → Senders">
    Under **Account**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click + Add Sender">
    Opens the create dialog.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enter the address">
    Must belong to a verified domain. Unverified domains are blocked.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enter a display name">
    The name recipients see as the sender (e.g. *Acme Reports Team*).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    The sender is now selectable on any automation or email default.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Who sees what

Emails from a verified sender display with your agency name in the recipient's inbox — no "sent via Oviond" footer, no mismatched from-address warnings, and SPF/DKIM pass cleanly because the domain is set up correctly.

See [Email Domains](/email-settings/domains) for the domain verification flow.


# SMTP
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/email-settings/smtp

Route Oviond emails through your own SMTP server.

By default Oviond sends automation emails via our managed provider (Resend). If your agency needs every email to pass through its own SMTP server — for audit, deliverability isolation, or IP warming — configure SMTP under **Account → Email Settings → SMTP**.

## Configure

<Steps>
  <Step title="Enable Use SMTP">
    Toggles Oviond from the managed provider to your SMTP server.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enter SMTP details">
    Host, port, username, password, and TLS/SSL setting. Most agencies use port 587 with STARTTLS.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    Oviond stores the credentials encrypted and starts using them for every automation delivery.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Test

Always test before going live:

<Steps>
  <Step title="Click Send Test Email">
    Under the SMTP config section.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Check delivery">
    Oviond sends a test email to your logged-in address via your configured SMTP. If it arrives, you're good.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Check the error if it fails">
    The error is shown inline — usually auth, TLS, or firewall-related.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Fall back

Toggle **Use SMTP** off to revert to Oviond's managed provider. No changes to existing automations are needed — they pick up the new route on the next fire.

<Warning>
  A misconfigured SMTP server stops every automation from delivering. Keep **Use SMTP** off until the test passes.
</Warning>


# Connect Google Analytics 4
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/guides/connect-ga4

End-to-end GA4 setup for a client — from OAuth to your first widget.

Google Analytics 4 is one of the most common data sources in Oviond reports. This guide walks through connecting GA4 to a client and dropping a first widget on a report.

## Before you start

* You have admin access (or higher) to the GA4 property you want to report on.
* You've already created an Oviond [client](/clients/add) for this customer.

## 1. Start the OAuth flow

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the client">
    Click the client in your sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open Data Sources">
    Inside the client workspace, click **Data Sources**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Google Analytics 4">
    Oviond opens a Google sign-in popup.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Authorize">
    Pick the Google account that has access to the GA4 property. Approve the scopes Oviond requests — these are read-only.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## 2. Pick the profile

GA4 connections can see every property the authorizing Google account has access to. You need to tell Oviond which property belongs to *this* client:

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the data source">
    Back in Data Sources, click the newly connected GA4 tile.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Select the property">
    From the dropdown, pick the GA4 property for this client. See [Profiles](/data-sources/profiles).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Rename the connection">
    Optionally rename the connection to something client-friendly (e.g. *Acme — Main Site*) so your team picks the right one when building widgets.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## 3. Test the connection

Before building widgets, confirm the connection works:

<Steps>
  <Step title="Click Test Connection">
    From the data source's three-dot menu. See [Test Connection](/data-sources/test).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Look for Success">
    A green tick means the OAuth token is live and GA4 is returning data.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## 4. Build your first GA4 widget

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open a project">
    Create a new [project](/projects/add) or open an existing one.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Add a KPI widget">
    Click **+ Add Widget** → **KPI**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick Google Analytics 4">
    In the Data Source dropdown, select the GA4 connection you just wired up.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick a metric">
    Start with **Sessions** — a universal GA4 KPI that exists for every property.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set the date range">
    **Last 30 Days (relative)** — this way the widget auto-updates every time the report is opened.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    Click **Save** in the top-right. The widget renders live data.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Common follow-ups

* Add a **Sessions by Source / Medium** table for traffic breakdown.
* Add a **Conversions** KPI if you've set up GA4 conversion events.
* Use **Calculated Metrics** to compute engagement rate or conversion rate.

## Troubleshooting

* **"No data returned"** — the property is connected but empty. Double-check the profile selection and the date range.
* **"Integration error"** — the OAuth token expired. Run [Test Connection](/data-sources/test), and if it fails, click **Reconnect**.
* **"Property not found"** — the authorizing Google account lost access to the property in GA4. Re-authorize with a Google account that still has access.


# Build Your First Report
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/guides/first-report

A complete walkthrough from creating a client to publishing a shared link.

This guide takes you from a fresh Oviond account to a live, shareable report for your first client. Expect it to take \~20 minutes end-to-end.

## Before you start

* You've signed up for Oviond and completed the [onboarding](/onboarding/overview) (full name, company name, website, client count).
* You have OAuth access to at least one data source (Google Ads, Google Analytics 4, Facebook Ads, etc).

## 1. Add a client

See [Add Client](/clients/add). At minimum, enter the client's **name** and **website**. Oviond auto-generates a thumbnail from the website URL.

## 2. Connect a data source

From the client's workspace:

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Data Sources">
    Inside the client, click **Data Sources** in the sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick an integration">
    Click a tile — e.g. Google Analytics 4 — and run the OAuth flow. See [Connect](/data-sources/connect).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick the profile">
    Select which GA4 property belongs to this client. See [Profiles](/data-sources/profiles).
  </Step>
</Steps>

## 3. Create the report

See [Add Project](/projects/add). Pick **Report** as the type (not Dashboard) and name it *Acme — Monthly Performance* or similar. Start from a [template](/templates/apply) if one matches your needs, otherwise blank.

## 4. Add pages and widgets

<Steps>
  <Step title="Add a page">
    The project starts with one page. See [Pages](/projects/pages) to add and rename.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Add your first widget">
    Click **+ Add Widget**. Pick a **KPI tile** for a quick win — e.g. *Total Sessions, Last 30 Days*.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Add a chart">
    Next, a **line chart** showing Sessions trended over the last 30 days — it pairs well with the KPI above.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    Click **Save** in the top-right. See [Widgets](/projects/widgets).
  </Step>
</Steps>

## 5. Apply a theme

To make the report feel on-brand:

<Steps>
  <Step title="Create a theme">
    See [Add Theme](/themes/add). Set your client's primary color — Oviond auto-derives the full chart palette.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Apply it to the project">
    See [Apply Theme](/themes/apply).
  </Step>
</Steps>

## 6. Share it

See [Share Project](/projects/share). Enable the public link, copy the short URL, and send it to your client. The client opens the link in any browser — no Oviond account needed.

## Next steps

* [Schedule a Monthly Report](/guides/monthly-report) — automate delivery to your client's inbox.
* [Connect Google Analytics 4](/guides/connect-ga4) — the full GA4-specific setup.
* [Templates](/templates/add) — save this report's layout so you can spin up the same thing for your next client in minutes.


# Monthly Client Report
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/guides/monthly-report

Automate a recurring monthly report email for a client.

This guide sets up a monthly automated report that delivers to your client's inbox on the 1st of every month. You'll end with an automation that runs without any manual work month to month.

## Before you start

* You have a [project](/projects/add) — report or dashboard — ready to send.
* You've [configured email sending](/email-settings/defaults) from your agency's domain (optional but recommended).
* You've tested the project's widgets with live data.

## 1. Verify the report is ready to send

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the project">
    Make sure every widget is configured against the right data source and date range.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Use a relative date range">
    Configure the project's default date range to something like **Last Month** or **Previous Calendar Month**. See [Update Project](/projects/update). Relative dates are essential for automations — each monthly fire should pull its own month's data.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Export a test PDF">
    See [PDF Export](/automations/pdf). Check that the rendered PDF looks clean before automating.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## 2. Create the automation

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Automations">
    From the left sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click + New Automation">
    See [Add Automation](/automations/add).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick the project">
    The report you just validated.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set the schedule">
    Frequency: **Monthly**. Day: **1st**. Time: a morning hour in the client's timezone.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set recipients">
    Add the client contact's email. You can include multiple recipients — they all receive the same message.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Configure the email">
    Subject:&#x20;*&#x20;—&#x20;*. Use the default sender from [Email Settings](/email-settings/defaults) or pick a specific one. Write a short intro paragraph for the body.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose PDF attachment">
    Attach the report as a PDF so the client has a durable copy.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Create">
    The automation is saved — it fires on the next scheduled run.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## 3. Test before going live

Always test:

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the automation">
    From the Automations list.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Test Send">
    See [Test Automation](/automations/test). Override the recipient to send to yourself.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Check the email">
    Subject, sender, body copy, and PDF attachment — all should look exactly how the client will see it.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## 4. Monitor the first real run

After the first scheduled delivery (on the 1st of next month):

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the automation's Run History">
    See [Run History](/automations/history).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Check the status">
    **Delivered** — you're good. **Partial** — some recipients bounced; check their email addresses. **Failed** — the render errored; read the message.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Check Email Logs">
    See [Email Logs](/email-settings/logs) for per-recipient delivery events (opened, clicked, bounced).
  </Step>
</Steps>

## If the client changes their address

Edit the automation, update the recipients list, and save. No need to re-test unless you also changed the sender or subject.

## If you need to pause

Toggle **Pause** on the automation row. The schedule stops firing until you unpause — no need to delete the automation. See [List Automations](/automations/list).


# Overview
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/guides/overview

End-to-end walkthroughs for the most common Oviond workflows.

The **Guides** tab collects step-by-step walkthroughs that cross multiple features — the kind of docs where a single feature page isn't enough. Use these when you want the full flow, screenshots, and decision points for a complete job.

## What's here

<CardGroup>
  <Card title="Build Your First Report" icon="rocket" href="/guides/first-report">
    From creating a client to publishing your first shared link.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Connect Google Analytics 4" icon="chart-line" href="/guides/connect-ga4">
    End-to-end GA4 setup — OAuth, profile selection, widgets.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Monthly Client Report" icon="calendar" href="/guides/monthly-report">
    Automate a scheduled email report for a recurring client.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## Who guides are for

* **New users** — pick *Build Your First Report* as your first read after the Quickstart.
* **Teams onboarding a new client** — the Monthly Client Report guide covers the full client-setup flow.
* **Debugging** — when a feature doesn't quite behave the way you expect, the guides show how it fits into the larger workflow.

For single-feature reference, use the **Documentation** tab's per-feature pages.


# Introduction
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/introduction

White-label marketing reporting for agencies. Build branded dashboards, connect 30+ data sources, and automate client report delivery.

Oviond is a white-label digital marketing reporting platform built for agencies. Connect your clients' marketing accounts — Google Ads, Facebook, and 30+ other platforms — and build professional dashboards and reports that you can deliver under your own brand. This documentation covers everything you need to get up and running, from creating your first report to automating scheduled delivery for your entire client base.

<CardGroup>
  <Card title="Quick Start" icon="rocket" href="/quickstart">
    Create your account and build your first report in minutes.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Authentication" icon="key" href="/authentication">
    Learn how to authenticate API requests with your API key.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Data Sources" icon="plug" href="/data-sources/list">
    Connect Google Ads, Facebook, and 30+ marketing platforms.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Custom Domains" icon="palette" href="/custom-domains/list">
    Set up your custom domain and brand for client-facing reports.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Projects" icon="chart-bar" href="/projects/list">
    Build dashboards and reports with drag-and-drop widgets.
  </Card>

  <Card title="API Reference" icon="code" href="/api/introduction">
    Explore the full Oviond REST API.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>

## How Oviond works

<Steps>
  <Step title="Connect your data sources">
    Link your clients' marketing accounts — Google Ads, Facebook Ads, Google Analytics, and more — through OAuth in a few clicks.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Build reports and dashboards">
    Use the drag-and-drop builder to create pages with charts, tables, and KPI widgets that pull live data from your connected integrations.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Apply your brand">
    Customize the logo, colors, and fonts on every report. Set up a custom domain so clients experience your agency's brand, not Oviond's.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Automate delivery">
    Schedule automated report emails or PDF exports to go out on a recurring basis — daily, weekly, or monthly.
  </Step>
</Steps>


# Authentication
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/mcp/auth

How the Oviond MCP server authenticates AI agents — OAuth 2.1 under the hood.

The Oviond MCP server uses **OAuth 2.1 authorization-code flow with PKCE** — the same pattern Anthropic's MCP spec mandates for remote servers. This section walks through what the agent and Oviond do during the handshake.

## Flow at a glance

1. **Discovery** — the agent fetches `https://api.oviond.com/.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` to learn the auth endpoints.
2. **Client registration** — the agent dynamically registers with Oviond (no manual client-secret setup required).
3. **Authorization request** — the agent opens a browser to Oviond's `/oauth/authorize` with a PKCE challenge.
4. **User consent** — you sign in to Oviond and approve the connection scope.
5. **Token exchange** — the agent exchanges the authorization code for an access token via `/oauth/token`.
6. **Tool calls** — every subsequent MCP request carries `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.

## Token scope

Access tokens are scoped to **the signed-in user's role and allowed projects**. The MCP server:

* Sees only clients, projects, and data the authenticated user can see in the web app.
* Inherits role restrictions (read-only users can only read via MCP).
* Can't delete or modify data the user isn't authorized for.

## Token lifetime

* **Access tokens** last 1 hour.
* **Refresh tokens** are issued alongside the access token and last 30 days.
* The MCP client auto-refreshes — you won't be prompted to sign in again during normal usage.

## Revoking access

Revoke an MCP client's access at any time from **Account → Connected Apps** in Oviond. Revoked tokens stop working immediately — the agent will need to re-authorize on its next tool call.

<Note>
  The MCP server does **not** use the Bearer API keys you create under **API Keys**. Those are for direct REST API calls. MCP has its own OAuth flow with per-client tokens.
</Note>


# Connect
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/mcp/connect

Wire Claude, Cursor, or another MCP client to your Oviond account.

Every MCP client supports remote HTTP servers with OAuth. The specifics differ, but the server URL is always the same:

```
https://api.oviond.com/mcp
```

## Claude Desktop

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Claude Desktop settings">
    From the menu bar, open **Settings → Connectors**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Add Oviond">
    Click **+ Add custom connector** and enter the URL `https://api.oviond.com/mcp`.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Sign in">
    Claude Desktop opens a browser window to Oviond's sign-in page. Sign in and authorize the connection.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Verify">
    Back in Claude Desktop, you should see Oviond in your connector list. Start a chat and ask Claude to *"list my Oviond clients"* to confirm.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Cursor

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Cursor settings">
    **Settings → MCP** (or edit `~/.cursor/mcp.json` directly).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Add the server">
    ```json theme={null}
    {
      "mcpServers": {
        "oviond": {
          "url": "https://api.oviond.com/mcp"
        }
      }
    }
    ```
  </Step>

  <Step title="Authorize">
    Restart Cursor. On first tool call, Cursor opens a browser to complete OAuth.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Other MCP clients

Any client that speaks the MCP HTTP transport with OAuth 2.1 support works. Point it at `https://api.oviond.com/mcp` and complete the OAuth flow.

<Note>
  Only **streamable HTTP** transport is supported. Stdio servers don't apply here — Oviond's MCP server is hosted by us, not run locally.
</Note>

## First prompts to try

* *"List all my Oviond clients."*
* *"Create a new client called 'Acme Corp' with website acme.example."*
* *"Build a dashboard called 'Paid Social Overview' for Acme."*
* *"Schedule the Acme monthly report to send on the 1st of every month."*


# Overview
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/mcp/overview

The Oviond MCP server lets Claude and other AI agents manage your Oviond account directly.

Oviond ships a **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** server so AI agents — Claude Desktop, Cursor, Claude Code, and any other MCP-compatible client — can manage your Oviond account programmatically. Ask your agent to *"create a new client and an agency report for them"* and it happens.

## What you can do

The MCP server exposes 19 tool categories that cover most of the Oviond surface area:

* **Clients & Folders** — create, rename, delete, and organize clients.
* **Projects & Pages** — create reports and dashboards, add pages, duplicate layouts.
* **Widgets** — add, update, and remove chart/KPI/table/text widgets.
* **Templates & Themes** — browse the template library, apply themes to projects.
* **Integrations** — list connected data sources, rename, and manage profiles.
* **Automations** — schedule and test automated report deliveries.
* **Branding & Company** — update logo, favicon, and company details.
* **Assets, Media, Domains, Search**, and more.

## How it works

* **Transport** — streamable HTTP at `https://api.oviond.com/mcp`.
* **Auth** — OAuth 2.1 authorization-code flow. Your AI agent launches a browser, you sign into Oviond, and the agent gets a scoped access token.
* **Isolation** — every tool call is scoped to the signed-in account. The agent can only touch data the authenticated user has permission for.

## Next steps

* [Connect](/mcp/connect) — wire up Claude, Cursor, or your MCP client to Oviond.
* [Authentication](/mcp/auth) — what the OAuth flow does under the hood.
* [Available Tools](/mcp/tools) — the full catalog of MCP tools.


# Available Tools
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/mcp/tools

The full catalog of tools the Oviond MCP server exposes.

The Oviond MCP server exposes 100+ tools across 19 categories. Each tool maps to one or more REST API operations — the MCP server is a thin wrapper that lets your AI agent call the Oviond API with natural language.

## Categories

| Category         | What the agent can do                                                              |
| ---------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Account**      | Read account info and usage.                                                       |
| **Agency**       | List and manage account-level reports not tied to a client.                        |
| **Assets**       | Manage saved widget sections.                                                      |
| **Automations**  | Create, pause, test, and delete scheduled deliveries.                              |
| **Branding**     | Update logo, favicon, and brand colors.                                            |
| **Clients**      | Create, rename, move, delete, and bulk-delete clients.                             |
| **Company**      | Update agency-level company info.                                                  |
| **Domain**       | Manage custom white-label domains.                                                 |
| **Folders**      | Create, rename, and delete client folders.                                         |
| **Integrations** | List connected data sources and their profiles.                                    |
| **Media**        | Upload, rename, and delete media files.                                            |
| **Pages**        | Add, reorder, rename, and delete pages inside projects.                            |
| **Projects**     | Create, duplicate, bulk-delete, and share reports/dashboards.                      |
| **Search**       | Search clients, projects, and assets by name.                                      |
| **Templates**    | Browse and apply templates.                                                        |
| **Themes**       | Create, update, and apply themes.                                                  |
| **Users**        | List, invite, update, and remove team members.                                     |
| **Widgets**      | Add every widget type (KPI, chart, table, text, image, embed, goal, button, data). |

## Conventions

* **Identifiers** — tool calls return resource IDs that the agent uses to chain subsequent calls. You never see the IDs in chat; the agent handles them behind the scenes.
* **Multi-tenancy** — every call is automatically scoped to the authenticated account. You don't pass an `account_id` or similar.
* **Rate limits** — the same per-account rate limits that apply to the REST API apply to MCP calls.

## Reference

Each MCP tool is a thin wrapper around a single REST operation. For the exact parameters and response shape, see the [API Reference](/api/introduction).


# Bulk Delete
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/media/bulk-delete

Soft-delete multiple media items at once.

Bulk delete is the fastest way to clean up leftover screenshots, duplicate uploads, or files from an offboarded client.

<Warning>
  Deleted media items are **soft-deleted** — the S3 file stays in place and the row is retained until the retention window expires. Widgets that reference a deleted item will show a broken image until the item is restored or re-wired to a different file. Review widget references first if you're removing a file currently in use.
</Warning>

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Media">
    From the left sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enter selection mode">
    Hover a tile and click the checkbox that appears, or toggle **Select** mode in the toolbar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick the items">
    Check every media item you want to delete. Select-all works per folder view.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Delete">
    Click **Delete selected** in the action bar. Items move to the Archive.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Undo a bulk delete

From the [Archive](/archive/view), filter to **Media**, select the items you just deleted, and click **Restore**. They reappear in the Media library with their original names and folders.


# Delete Media
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/media/delete

Soft-delete a single media item with restore available from the Archive.

Deleting a single media item removes it from the active library and moves it to the [Archive](/archive/view). The underlying S3 file is retained for the restore window — nothing is permanently destroyed at this step.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Media">
    From the left sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the item's menu">
    Click the three-dot menu on the tile.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Delete">
    Confirm when prompted. The item disappears from the library.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## What widgets see

Widgets that referenced the deleted item show a broken-image placeholder with a **Missing Media** badge. Options:

* [Restore](/archive/restore) the item from the Archive — the widget re-renders cleanly.
* Edit the widget and pick a different media item.

## Retention

Archived media items older than the retention window (90 days by default) are **permanently purged**, including the S3 file. Restore before then if you want to keep it.


# Folders
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/media/folders

Group related media files into folders.

Media folders keep a growing library navigable. Common patterns: one folder per client (*Acme — Logos*), per type (*Icons*, *Screenshots*), or per report (*Q1 Cover Images*).

<Note>
  Media folders are a separate system from [Client folders](/clients/folders). They only organize items inside the Media library — they don't scope media to any specific client.
</Note>

## Create a folder

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Media">
    From the left sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click + New Folder">
    In the Media sidebar or toolbar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Name the folder">
    Enter a descriptive name.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    The folder appears in the Media sidebar, ready to receive items.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Move items into a folder

From any media tile, click the three-dot menu and choose **Move to folder**, then pick the target. Multi-select also supports bulk move — check multiple tiles, then click **Move** in the action bar.

## Rename a folder

Click the three-dot menu next to a folder name and choose **Rename**.

## Delete a folder

Open the folder's three-dot menu and choose **Delete**.

<Note>
  Deleting a folder **does not delete the media items inside it**. Items return to the unorganized pool and remain accessible from **All Media**.
</Note>


# List Media
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/media/list

Browse every file in your Media library.

The Media library lists every uploaded file — images, logos, icons, and documents — in a grid view, most-recent-first. Click any item to preview it, copy its URL, or move it into a folder.

## Views

* **All Media** — the unfiltered grid.
* **By Folder** — narrow to a single [folder](/media/folders) from the sidebar.
* **Search** — match by file name.
* **Sort** — switch between recent activity, name, and file size.

## What's on a tile

| Element          | Description                                                     |
| ---------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Thumbnail**    | Rendered preview of the file.                                   |
| **Name**         | The file's display name — editable via [Rename](/media/update). |
| **Size**         | File size in KB/MB.                                             |
| **Folder badge** | The folder this item is in, if any.                             |

## Quick actions

From any tile's three-dot menu:

* **Preview** — open the full file in a lightbox.
* **Copy URL** — copy the direct file URL (useful for referencing in widgets or external tools).
* **Move to folder** — see [Folders](/media/folders).
* **Rename** — see [Rename Media](/media/update).
* **Delete** — see [Delete Media](/media/delete).


# Rename Media
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/media/update

Change the display name of a media file.

Renaming a media item updates its display name only. The underlying S3 file keeps the same URL — widgets referencing the item don't need to be re-wired.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Media">
    From the left sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the item's menu">
    Click the three-dot menu on the tile.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Rename">
    Type the new name and press **Enter**.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## What rename changes

* The display name shown in the library, in the widget picker, and in search.

## What rename doesn't change

* The file's URL (the S3 key is immutable).
* Any existing widget that references the file — it keeps working.
* The file size or mime type.

<Tip>
  Use consistent naming — e.g. `client-logo-acme.svg`, `client-logo-contoso.svg` — to make search and folder organization easier as your library grows.
</Tip>


# Upload Media
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/media/upload

Upload images and files into the Oviond Media library.

The **Media library** is where every image, logo, icon, and file referenced from widgets, reports, and share pages lives. Uploads go straight to S3-backed storage — Oviond generates a pre-signed URL, the file uploads directly from your browser, and the metadata row is committed once upload finishes.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Media library">
    Click **Media** in the left sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Upload">
    Opens a file picker — or drag files straight onto the Media grid.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick files">
    Images (PNG, JPG, SVG, WebP) and common file types are all supported. Multi-select works — every file uploads in parallel.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Optional: drop into a folder">
    If you opened Media from inside a folder, uploads land in that folder. Otherwise they go to the unorganized pool.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Supported formats

| Type     | Formats                  |
| -------- | ------------------------ |
| Image    | PNG, JPG, SVG, WebP, GIF |
| Document | PDF                      |

## Size limits

Individual files up to **25 MB**. For larger uploads, chunk them or host externally and reference the URL directly from the widget.

<Tip>
  For logos and branding, prefer **SVG** — they stay crisp at every report resolution and render cleanly in PDF export.
</Tip>


# Preferences
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/notifications/preferences

Choose which notifications trigger email delivery.

**Preferences** are per-user — each team member controls which Oviond events reach their inbox. In-app notifications are always on account-wide; email delivery is the toggle you manage here.

## Event types

| Event                        | Default email | Notes                                                         |
| ---------------------------- | ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Automation failed**        | On            | Email when an automation errors during a scheduled run.       |
| **Integration disconnected** | On            | Email when a data source's OAuth token is revoked or expires. |
| **Team invite**              | On            | Email when a user is invited to your account.                 |
| **Team member removed**      | On            | Email when a team member is removed.                          |
| **PDF export ready**         | Off           | Email when an on-demand PDF is generated.                     |
| **Weekly digest**            | Off           | Summary of activity each Monday.                              |

## How to change preferences

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Notifications → Preferences">
    From the sidebar (or click your avatar → Preferences).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Toggle each event">
    Turn email delivery on or off per event type.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    Changes take effect immediately.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  Preferences are per-user — switching them doesn't affect your teammates. In-app notifications (the bell icon) show every event for every user in the account regardless of email preferences.
</Note>


# View Notifications
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/notifications/view

In-app alerts and email digests from your Oviond account.

Oviond surfaces events as **Notifications** (the bell icon in the top bar) for things you need to react to:

* An integration disconnected and needs re-authentication
* An automation run failed
* A team member was invited or removed
* A PDF export finished

Each user can configure per-event email delivery under **Notifications → Preferences**. In-app notifications are shared account-wide; email delivery is per-user.


# Onboarding
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/onboarding/overview

The 4-step account setup shown after your first sign-in.

After your first sign-in, Oviond opens an **Account Setup** modal that asks for four things. The modal is blocking — you can't click outside or press Escape to dismiss — but each step is one field, and the whole flow takes under a minute.

Once you complete it, the `account.onboarding` flag flips off and the modal never appears again. You can always update the values later under [Account](/account/overview) and [Company](/company/overview).

## The 4 steps

<Steps>
  <Step title="Full Name">
    *"What should we call you?"* — your name as it should appear throughout the app and in activity-log entries. Also shown on team member listings.
  </Step>
