  <Step title="Company Name">
    *"What is the name of your company?"* — the agency name used on report headers, email footers, and shared links (unless overridden by [white-label branding](/company/branding)).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Website">
    *"What is your company's website?"* — your agency's URL. If you don't include `http://` or `https://`, Oviond adds `https://` automatically. Shown on the accounts row and used for the default screenshot on your own company card.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Client Count">
    *"How many clients do you report for?"* — pick one: **1–5**, **6–20**, **21–100**, or **100+**. This isn't a hard limit — it's used for plan recommendations and to tailor onboarding emails.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## What happens next

When you click **Get Started** on step 4, Oviond:

* Saves your name to [Profile Settings](/users/profile).
* Saves company name and website to [Company](/company/overview).
* Records the client-count bucket on your account.
* Sets `account.onboarding = false` so the modal doesn't show again.
* Drops you on your Clients dashboard, ready to [add your first client](/clients/add).

## Troubleshooting

* **The modal won't dismiss** — each step validates the field before you can advance. Fix any red inline error, then **Continue** becomes active.
* **Website rejected** — it expects a simple domain (e.g. `yourcompany.com`). Leave off paths and trailing slashes.
* **The modal reappears on a different device** — it follows the account, not the device. If you only partially completed it elsewhere, finish it on this device and it syncs.


# Add Project
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/projects/add

Create a new report or dashboard inside a client's workspace.

A **project** is a named report or dashboard that lives inside a specific client's workspace. Every project holds its own pages and widgets and can be shared publicly via a link.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the client">
    From your Clients list, click the client you want to create the project under. Everything you create from inside the client's workspace is automatically scoped to that client — you don't pick a client again.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click + New Project">
    From the client's Projects tab. Opens the new-project dialog.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Name your project">
    The name appears on the shared link, in PDF exports, and in the email subject when the project is automated.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick the type">
    Choose **Report** (paged report) or **Dashboard** (single-scroll live view).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Start blank or from a template">
    Starting from a [template](/templates/list) pre-populates pages and widgets you can customize. Otherwise start blank.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Create">
    Click **Create**. Oviond opens the project editor.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Tip>
  Need an account-level project that isn't tied to a single client? See [Agency → Add Project](/agency/add).
</Tip>


# Archive Project
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/projects/archive

Soft-delete a project with full restore within the retention window.

Archiving a project removes it from your active Reports list without permanently destroying the project's pages, widgets, or date-range settings. You can restore the project from the [Archive](/archive/view) any time before the retention window expires.

## Archive a project

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the project menu">
    From the Reports screen, click the three-dot menu on the project card, or open the project and use its header menu.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Archive">
    The project disappears from the active Reports list and moves to the [Archive](/archive/view).
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Restore

From the Archive, find the project, click **Restore**, and it reappears under its original client with all pages and widgets intact.

## Retention

Archived projects older than the retention window (90 days by default) are **permanently purged** by a background job. For long-term storage of reference reports, [duplicate](/projects/duplicate) into a dedicated "Archive" client instead.


# Bulk Delete
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/projects/bulk-delete

Remove multiple projects at once.

Bulk delete speeds up cleanup when you're archiving a batch of old reports — for example, rolling over to a new fiscal year or removing drafts after a client offboards.

<Warning>
  Deleted projects move to the [Archive](/archive/view) along with all their pages and widgets. They can be restored within the retention window; after that, they're purged permanently. Export anything you need before proceeding.
</Warning>

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Reports screen">
    From the left sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enter selection mode">
    Hover a project card and click the checkbox that appears, or toggle **Select** mode from the toolbar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick the projects">
    Check every project you want to delete.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Delete">
    Click **Delete selected** in the action bar and confirm. Projects move to the Archive.
  </Step>
</Steps>


# Duplicate Project
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/projects/duplicate

Copy a project — pages, widgets, and settings — under a new name.

Duplicating creates an exact clone of a project, including every page, widget, and setting. Useful for:

* Reusing a reporting structure for a different client or month
* Creating a variant without editing the original
* Testing layout changes on a copy before applying to the live report

<Steps>
  <Step title="Find the project">
    On the Reports screen, locate the project you want to copy.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the project menu">
    Click the three-dot menu next to the project.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Duplicate">
    Oviond creates a copy named `Copy of [original]`.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Rename and reassign">
    Open the copy's settings to give it a new name and, if needed, assign it to a different client.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Tip>
  If you want a reusable starting point rather than a one-off copy, [create a Template](/templates/add) from the project instead — templates keep a live library you can re-apply to any future project.
</Tip>


# List Projects
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/projects/list

Browse, search, and filter every project in your workspace.

The **Reports** screen lists every project across every client. Use it to find, open, or manage any report or dashboard.

## Filter and search

* **Search** — type in the search bar to match by project name.
* **Client filter** — narrow to a single client using the **Client** dropdown.
* **Type filter** — show only **Reports** or only **Dashboards**.
* **Sort** — switch between recent activity, name, and date created.

## What's on a project card

| Element         | Description                                |
| --------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| **Name**        | Click the name to open the project editor. |
| **Client**      | Shows which client the project belongs to. |
| **Type**        | Report or Dashboard badge.                 |
| **Last edited** | Timestamp of the most recent save.         |

<Tip>
  Use consistent project naming — including month and year (e.g. *Acme — Paid Social — March 2026*) — to make the list searchable as it grows.
</Tip>

## Quick actions

From any project card's three-dot menu:

* **Open** — enter the editor.
* **Duplicate** — see [Duplicate Project](/projects/duplicate).
* **Share** — see [Share Project](/projects/share).
* **Archive** — see [Archive Project](/projects/archive).


# Pages
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/projects/pages

Organize a project into tabs — one page per section of the report.

A **page** is a tab inside a project. Each page has its own grid layout and holds any number of widgets. Viewers switch between pages to explore different sections — e.g. one page per marketing channel.

Every project starts with at least one page. Add more at any time.

## Add a page

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the project">
    Click the project on the Reports screen.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click + Add Page">
    The button sits in the page tab bar at the top of the editor.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Rename it">
    Double-click the tab to rename. Use clear names like *Google Ads — Overview* or *SEO Performance*.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Reorder and hide pages

* **Reorder** — drag a page tab left or right to change its position.
* **Hide** — from the page's three-dot menu, toggle **Hidden** to drop it from the client-facing view without deleting it.

## Delete a page

Open the page's three-dot menu and choose **Delete**. Every widget on the page is removed with the page.

<Tip>
  Plan pages before adding widgets. Grouping related data per page (e.g. one page per channel) keeps reports scannable for clients.
</Tip>


# Share Project
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/projects/share

Publish a project via a shareable public link.

Share a project with a client, stakeholder, or prospect via a public URL. The recipient opens the link in any browser — **no Oviond account required** — and sees the report with live data.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the project">
    From the Reports screen, click the project.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Share">
    The Share button sits in the top-right of the editor. The full link and a short link both get generated.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enable the public link">
    Toggle **Public link** on.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Copy the link">
    Copy the full URL or the short URL. Short URLs are easier to share in chat and email.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Optional: password-protect">
    Enable **Password** under the share settings and set a passphrase. Recipients are prompted for it before the report loads.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Custom domain

If you've set up a [Custom Domain](/custom-domains/list), shared links use your white-label domain instead of `app.oviond.com`. Your logo, favicon, and brand colors render on the share page out of the box.

## Live vs snapshot

Public links render live data every time they're opened. To send a static snapshot (e.g. end-of-month), use [PDF Export](/automations/pdf) instead.

<Warning>
  Anyone with the link can view the report. Use password protection if the report contains sensitive client data.
</Warning>


# Update Project
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/projects/update

Rename, reassign, and configure project settings.

Project settings cover everything outside the report canvas — name, client assignment, date range defaults, and PDF export options.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the project">
    From the Reports screen, click the project you want to edit.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open project settings">
    Click the **Settings** icon in the editor or choose **Project Settings** from the project's three-dot menu.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Rename">
    Edit the **Project name** field. The new name immediately appears on the shared link and PDF exports.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Reassign the client">
    Use the **Client** dropdown to move the project to a different client.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set the default date range">
    Pick a fixed range or a relative range (like *Last 30 Days*) — every widget that uses the project-level date inherits this.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Configure PDF export">
    Under **PDF**, set paper size, orientation, and any header / footer content for exports. See [PDF Export](/automations/pdf).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    Click **Save** to apply.
  </Step>
</Steps>


# Widgets
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/projects/widgets

Charts, KPI tiles, tables, and text blocks that populate every page.

A **widget** is an individual visualization on a page. Each widget connects to a data source and displays one metric or breakdown.

## Widget types

| Type            | Use for                                                           |
| --------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **KPI tile**    | A single headline metric, optional period-over-period comparison. |
| **Chart**       | Trends over time — line, bar, or area.                            |
| **Table**       | Multiple metrics or dimensions side by side.                      |
| **Pie / donut** | Breakdown of a metric by dimension (e.g. traffic by source).      |
| **Text block**  | Freeform text — headings, commentary, summaries.                  |
| **Image**       | Inline images from the Media library or a URL.                    |
| **Embed**       | Embed any iframe-compatible source.                               |
| **Goal**        | Progress bar toward a target value.                               |
| **Button**      | A CTA link (e.g. to a booking page).                              |

## Add a widget

<Steps>
  <Step title="Select the page">
    Click the page tab you want to add the widget to.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the widget panel">
    Click **+ Add Widget** in the toolbar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose the type">
    Pick KPI, Chart, Table, etc.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick a data source">
    Choose the [Data Source](/data-sources/list) the widget pulls from. Only sources connected to this client are available.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick a metric">
    Select the metric (and optional dimension).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Set the date range">
    Fixed or relative (e.g. *Last 30 Days*) — relative ranges auto-update whenever the report is viewed.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Add to the page">
    Click **Add**. The widget appears on the canvas.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Configure a widget

Click any widget to open its settings panel on the right:

* **Data source / metric / dimension** — swap what the widget displays.
* **Date range** — override the project-level default for this widget.
* **Chart type** — switch visualizations without losing your data config.
* **Appearance** — title, colors, per-type display options.

## Rearrange and resize

Widgets use a drag-and-drop grid:

* **Move** — drag the widget header to a new position.
* **Resize** — drag the corner handle. Widgets snap to the grid.

## Bulk delete widgets

Hold **Shift** and click each widget to multi-select, then click **Delete selected** in the toolbar.

## Saving

Oviond uses an atomic save — **every page and every widget in the project** is saved in one operation. Click **Save** in the top-right of the editor before leaving.

<Warning>
  Navigating away without saving loses every unsaved widget change. Always save first.
</Warning>


# Get Started
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/quickstart

Create your Oviond account, connect a marketing data source, and publish your first client report in five steps. Everything you need to get started.

Oviond is a white-label marketing reporting platform for agencies. This quickstart walks you through signing up, connecting a marketing data source, and building your first report so you can see live client data in minutes.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Create your account">
    Go to [app.oviond.com](https://app.oviond.com) and click **Sign Up**. Enter your name, email, and password, then verify your email address from the confirmation message Oviond sends you.

    <Note>
      If you're joining an existing agency account, ask your admin to send you an invitation instead of signing up directly.
    </Note>
  </Step>

  <Step title="Complete onboarding">
    On your first sign-in, Oviond opens a 4-step setup modal: your full name, company name, company website, and how many clients you report for. The whole flow takes under a minute and unlocks the main app. See [Onboarding](/onboarding/overview) for what each step does.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Add your first client">
    Go to **Clients** in the left sidebar and click **Add Client**. Enter the client name and any optional details. Clients are the top-level container that groups all their reports and connected data sources together.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Connect a data source">
    Inside your new client, click **Connect Integration**. Choose a platform (for example, Google Ads or Facebook Ads) and follow the OAuth authorization flow. Oviond will request read-only access to the account's data.

    See [Connecting Data Sources](/data-sources/connect) for a full list of supported integrations and connection steps.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Create a report">
    Go to **Projects** and click **New Project**. Give it a name, select the client you just created, and choose a template or start from a blank canvas.

    Drag widgets onto your report page from the right-hand panel. Each widget is tied to a metric and data source — select the integration you connected in the previous step.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Share with your client">
    Click **Share** in the top toolbar. You can copy a public link your client can view without logging in, or schedule automated email delivery. To apply your agency's branding, see [White Label Overview](/custom-domains/list).
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Next steps

<CardGroup>
  <Card title="Connect more integrations" icon="plug" href="/data-sources/list">
    Add Google Analytics, Facebook, LinkedIn, and 30+ other platforms.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Set up white labeling" icon="palette" href="/custom-domains/list">
    Use your own domain and branding for client-facing reports.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Automate report delivery" icon="clock" href="/automations/scheduled">
    Schedule recurring PDF or email reports for your clients.
  </Card>

  <Card title="Invite team members" icon="users" href="/users/list">
    Add your colleagues to collaborate on reports.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>


# Add Template
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/templates/add

Save a project as a reusable starting point.

A **template** is a saved project scaffold — pages, widgets, date range, and settings — that you can apply to any new project. Templates save you from rebuilding the same report structure for every new client.

## Create from an existing project

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the source project">
    The project whose layout you want to reuse.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Save as Template">
    From the project's three-dot menu. Oviond copies the pages and widgets (minus the data values) into a new template.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Name and describe it">
    Give it a clear name (e.g. *Paid Social Monthly*) and an optional description so your team knows when to pick it.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Optional: upload a thumbnail">
    A thumbnail helps teammates find the right template at a glance.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    The template appears in the [Templates library](/templates/list) and is available to every new project.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## What gets saved

* Every page and its layout
* Every widget's type, data-source key, metric, dimension, and display settings
* Project-level date range and PDF export settings

## What doesn't

* Client-specific data connections — these are re-wired per project when the template is applied
* Live values — the template holds the structure, not the data


# Apply Template
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/templates/apply

Use a template as the starting point for a new project.

Applying a template copies its pages, widgets, and settings into a brand-new project. You then wire the widgets to the client's specific data sources.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Start a new project">
    From the Reports screen, click **+ New Project**. See [Add Project](/projects/add).
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Use a Template">
    In the create dialog, pick **Start from template** instead of **Blank**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick a template">
    Browse **My Templates** or the **Oviond Library**. Preview any template to see the page and widget layout before applying.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Assign a client & create">
    Assign the project to a client and click **Create**. Oviond copies every page and widget into the new project.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Wire data sources">
    Open each widget and pick the matching data source for your client — the template ships with the widget structure but no live connection.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Tip>
  If a template expects a data source you haven't connected for this client, Oviond flags the widget with a **Connect required** badge. See [Connect Data Source](/data-sources/connect).
</Tip>


# Delete Template
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/templates/delete

Remove a template from your library.

Deleting a template removes it from the Templates library. Projects that were created from the template **keep** their pages and widgets — removing the template doesn't touch any project that was already built from it.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Templates screen">
    From the left sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Find the template">
    Use search or filters to locate it.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the template menu">
    Click the three-dot menu on the template card.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Delete">
    Confirm when prompted. The template is permanently removed from your library.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  Templates in the **Oviond Library** are not deletable from your account — they're shared across every customer and maintained by Oviond.
</Note>


# List Templates
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/templates/list

Browse your account's templates and the shared Oviond library.

The **Templates** screen shows every template available to you — those you've created yourself, and the shared library of templates Oviond provides out of the box.

## Sources

| Source             | What it contains                                                                 |
| ------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **My Templates**   | Templates you or your team created from your own projects.                       |
| **Oviond Library** | Shared templates maintained by Oviond — usable by every account without copying. |

## Filters

* **Search** — match by template name.
* **Data sources** — filter to templates that use specific integrations (e.g. templates that include Google Ads widgets).
* **Type** — Reports only or Dashboards only.
* **Sort** — by ranking, name, or creation date.

## What's on a template card

| Element                | Description                                   |
| ---------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| **Thumbnail**          | Preview image uploaded with the template.     |
| **Name & description** | Short summary of what the template is for.    |
| **Data-source badges** | Which integrations the widgets inside expect. |

Click a template to preview it before applying to a project — see [Apply Template](/templates/apply).


# Add Theme
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/themes/add

Create a reusable color + font palette for reports and dashboards.

A **theme** is a reusable bundle of visual tokens — primary color, ten chart colors, font, border radius, and shadow style — that can be applied to any project or client.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Themes screen">
    From the left sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click + New Theme">
    Opens the theme editor.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Name the theme">
    Use a clear name like *Acme Brand* or *Dark Pitch Deck*.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick a primary color">
    Oviond auto-derives a ten-chart color palette from this. You can override any chart color individually.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose typography">
    Pick a font, border radius, and shadow style.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    The theme appears in your Themes library and becomes available in every project's theme picker.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Tip>
  The auto-derived chart palette works for most brands, but if your brand has a mandated full color spec, override each of the ten chart colors manually before saving.
</Tip>


# Apply Theme
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/themes/apply

Use a theme on a project or set it as a client's default.

Themes apply at two levels:

| Level           | What it affects                                                        |
| --------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Per-project** | Applies the theme only to the selected project.                        |
| **Per-client**  | Sets the theme as the default for every new project under that client. |

## Apply to a project

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the project">
    From the Reports screen.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open Project Settings">
    From the project's three-dot menu or the settings icon.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick a theme">
    Under **Theme**, select from My Themes or the Oviond Library.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    Every chart, KPI tile, and background re-renders using the theme's tokens.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Set a client default

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the client's workspace">
    From the Clients list.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open Client Settings">
    Click the three-dot menu on the client header.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick a default theme">
    Under **Default Theme**, select one. New projects under this client start with this theme.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  Changing a client's default theme does **not** change existing projects — only new ones. Update existing projects individually if you want them retroactively rethemed.
</Note>


# Delete Theme
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/themes/delete

Remove a theme from your library.

Deleting a theme removes it from your Themes library. Projects that used the theme fall back to Oviond's default theme — the projects themselves are untouched.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Themes screen">
    From the left sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the theme menu">
    Click the three-dot menu on the theme card.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Delete">
    Confirm when prompted.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  Themes from the **Oviond Library** aren't deletable from your account — they're shared and maintained by Oviond.
</Note>

<Warning>
  If the theme is set as a client's **default**, the client's default falls back to Oviond's standard theme. Already-created projects keep their own theme settings.
</Warning>


# List Themes
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/themes/list

Browse every theme in your account and in the Oviond library.

The **Themes** screen shows every theme you can apply to a project or client.

## Sources

| Source             | What it contains                             |
| ------------------ | -------------------------------------------- |
| **My Themes**      | Themes you created.                          |
| **Oviond Library** | Neutral starter themes maintained by Oviond. |

## What's on a theme card

| Element          | Description                                          |
| ---------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| **Name**         | Shown in pickers and in the project sidebar.         |
| **Color swatch** | Primary color + first few chart colors as a preview. |
| **Font preview** | Sample text in the theme's font.                     |

## Quick actions

From a theme card's three-dot menu:

* **Edit** — see [Update Theme](/themes/update).
* **Apply** — see [Apply Theme](/themes/apply).
* **Delete** — see [Delete Theme](/themes/delete).


# Update Theme
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/themes/update

Edit an existing theme's colors, fonts, or styling tokens.

Updating a theme changes **every project** that uses it. Project-level data isn't affected — just the visual tokens.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Themes screen">
    From the left sidebar.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the theme editor">
    Click the theme, or choose **Edit** from its three-dot menu.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Change tokens">
    Update any of: name, primary color, chart colors, font, border radius, shadow.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Preview">
    The preview panel shows a sample chart and KPI tile using the current settings.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    The theme is re-applied to every project that uses it on next render.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Tip>
  If you change the **primary color** without manually overriding the chart palette, Oviond re-derives the chart colors from the new primary. Override individual chart colors before saving if you need them pinned.
</Tip>


# Invite User
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/users/invite

Add a new team member to your Oviond account.

Invite a colleague to collaborate on reports, connect integrations, or manage clients. The invitee receives an email with a link to set their password and sign in.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Users">
    From the sidebar under **Account → Users**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click + Invite User">
    Opens the invite dialog.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enter the email">
    Must be a real email address — the invite link goes there.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Pick a role">
    * **Owner** — full access to every feature, including account deletion. One per account.
    * **Admin** — manage users, billing, all clients, all projects.
    * **Projects** — limited to projects you explicitly allow via the **Allowed Projects** selector.
    * **Read-only** — view-only across the account.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Optional: restrict to specific projects">
    If the role is **Projects**, use the **Allowed Projects** picker to list which projects this user can see and edit.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Send invite">
    The user receives an email with a link to set their password.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  Invites don't expire, but the user must click the link to activate their account before they can sign in.
</Note>


# List Users
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/users/list

Every team member with access to this Oviond account.

Oviond lets you invite colleagues to your account so you can build and manage reports together. Each team member gets their own login and is assigned a role that controls what they can see and do inside your workspace.

To manage team members, go to **Account Settings** and select the **Team Members** tab.

<Note>
  The number of team members you can invite depends on your plan. If you've reached your seat limit, you'll need to upgrade before you can add more. See [Plans and billing](/billing/plans) for details.
</Note>

## Invite a team member

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Team Members tab">
    Go to **Account Settings** and click **Team Members**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Click Invite team member">
    Click the **Invite team member** button (or **Add member**) in the top-right corner.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Enter their email address">
    Type the email address of the person you want to invite.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Assign a role">
    Select the role that best fits their responsibilities. See [Team member roles](#team-member-roles) below for a breakdown of each role.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Send the invitation">
    Click **Send invitation**. The invitee receives an email with a link to accept the invitation and set up their login. The invitation link expires after a set period — if they don't accept in time, you can resend it from the team member list.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  Invited members appear in your team list with a **Pending** status until they accept the invitation and complete their account setup.
</Note>

## Team member roles

Oviond provides distinct roles to give you control over what each person can access and modify.

| Role        | Description                                                                                                                             |
| ----------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Admin**   | Full access to the account. Can manage team members, billing, account settings, white-label configuration, and all clients and reports. |
| **Manager** | Can create and manage clients, reports, and integrations. Cannot access billing or account-level settings.                              |
| **Editor**  | Can edit reports and dashboards for clients they're assigned to. Cannot create or delete clients or manage integrations.                |
| **Viewer**  | Read-only access. Can view reports and dashboards but cannot make any changes.                                                          |

<Note>
  The account owner always retains full admin access and cannot be removed by other team members.
</Note>

## Edit a team member's role

You can change a team member's role at any time. Changes take effect immediately.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Team Members tab">
    Go to **Account Settings** and click **Team Members**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Locate the team member">
    Find the team member in the list.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Edit their role">
    Click the **Edit** icon (or the three-dot menu) next to their name and select **Edit role**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Select a new role">
    Choose the updated role from the dropdown.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save the change">
    Click **Save**. The team member's permissions update immediately — they will see the new access level the next time they perform an action or refresh their session.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Resend an invitation

If a pending team member hasn't accepted their invitation, you can resend it.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Find the pending team member">
    In the **Team Members** tab, look for members with a **Pending** status.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Resend the invitation">
    Click the three-dot menu next to their name and select **Resend invitation**. A new invitation email is sent to their address.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## Remove a team member

Removing a team member revokes their access to your Oviond workspace immediately. Their login is deactivated and they will not be able to access any reports, clients, or settings.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open the Team Members tab">
    Go to **Account Settings** and click **Team Members**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Locate the team member">
    Find the person you want to remove.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Select Remove">
    Click the three-dot menu next to their name and select **Remove member**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Confirm removal">
    Confirm the action in the dialog that appears. The team member is removed immediately and their seat becomes available for a new invite.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
  Removing a team member does not delete any reports or clients they created. All content they built remains in your workspace.
</Note>


# Profile Settings
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/users/profile

Update your own name, avatar, email, and preferences.

Profile settings are personal to your login — every user edits their own, regardless of role.

## What you can change

| Setting           | Notes                                                                                                            |
| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Full name**     | Shown throughout the app and in activity-log entries.                                                            |
| **Avatar**        | Upload any square image. Displayed in the sidebar and on shared pages when you're the account's primary contact. |
| **Email address** | Changing your email re-verifies it — Oviond sends a confirmation link to the new address.                        |
| **Language**      | UI language — see the [supported languages](#supported-languages) below.                                         |
| **Theme**         | Light / Dark / System. Doesn't affect how reports render for clients.                                            |

## How to access

Click your avatar or initials in the bottom-left of the sidebar, then **Profile**.

## Supported languages

Oviond's UI ships in 14 languages. Pick the one that matches your team's preference from the **Language** dropdown — the entire app (navigation, buttons, forms, tooltips) re-renders immediately.

| Code    | Language                 |
| ------- | ------------------------ |
| `en`    | English (default)        |
| `af-ZA` | Afrikaans (South Africa) |
| `bg`    | Bulgarian                |
| `da`    | Danish                   |
| `de`    | German                   |
| `el`    | Greek                    |
| `es`    | Spanish                  |
| `fr`    | French                   |
| `it`    | Italian                  |
| `nl`    | Dutch                    |
| `pl`    | Polish                   |
| `pt`    | Portuguese               |
| `ro`    | Romanian                 |
| `tr`    | Turkish                  |

<Note>
  The language setting is **per-user** — each team member can pick their own, and what you choose doesn't affect how client-facing reports render. Reports always display in the language they were authored in.
</Note>

<Note>
  Your role, allowed projects, and account-level settings are managed by an Admin or Owner — not from your profile. See [Update User](/users/update).
</Note>


# Remove User
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/users/remove

Revoke a team member's access to your Oviond account.

Removing a user immediately revokes their access. They can no longer sign in, and any API keys they issued stop working.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Users">
    Under **Account → Users**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the user's menu">
    Click the three-dot menu.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Remove">
    Confirm when prompted.
  </Step>
</Steps>

## What happens to their work

* **Reports and dashboards they created** stay in the account — they belong to the client, not the user.
* **Integrations they connected** keep working — the OAuth tokens are stored on the account, not the user.
* **API keys they issued** are revoked immediately. If any automations depend on them, regenerate the keys under a different user or the owner.
* **Activity log entries** remain intact with their name attached for audit purposes.

<Warning>
  Removing the only account **Owner** requires transferring ownership first. See [Update User](/users/update).
</Warning>


# Update User
Source: https://docs.oviond.com/users/update

Change a team member's role, name, or allowed projects.

Only the account **Owner** or **Admin** role can update another user's settings.

<Steps>
  <Step title="Open Users">
    Under **Account → Users**.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Open the user's menu">
    Click the three-dot menu next to the user's row.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Choose Edit">
    Opens the user editor.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Update the role">
    Change between Owner, Admin, Projects, or Read-only. See [Invite User](/users/invite) for role definitions.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Update allowed projects (Projects role only)">
    For users with the **Projects** role, pick exactly which projects they can see.
  </Step>

  <Step title="Save">
    Changes take effect on the user's next page load.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Warning>
  Demoting the **Owner** moves ownership to whoever you promote — you can't leave an account ownerless. To transfer ownership, first make the target user an Admin, then re-invite yourself as Admin and ask the new Owner to demote you.
</Warning>
